name: 107.3f x card
description: 107.3f - Sometimes X appears in the text of a spell or ability but not in a mana cost, alternative cost, additional cost, or activation cost. If the value of X isn’t defined, the controller of the spell or ability chooses the value of X at the appropriate time.

name: 40k model
description: Cards named after and representing a Warhammer 40,000 model.

name: 5c set mechanic commander
description: A five-color commander designed to support one or more themes and/or mechanics in a set.

name: ablative armor
description: Lose counters instead of taking damage

name: abrade
description: Modal instant or sorcery spells that offer the option to either deal damage to creature(s) or remove artifact(s).

name: absorb
description: A static ability preventing a fixed number of damage to permanents or players.

name: abyss
description: Recurring effects that make players sacrifice, destroy or otherwise remove permanents, usually creatures, every turn.

name: activate from exile
description: Abilities that can be activated from exile.

name: activate from graveyard
description: Abilities that can be activated from inside a graveyard.

name: activate from hand
description: Activated abilities that can be activated from your hand.

name: activate from stack
description: Abilities that can be activated from an object on the stack.

name: addendum
description: Instant-speed cards that do something extra if you played them during your main phase.

name: adds multiple mana
description: Cards that add more than one mana at a time.

name: aesthetic counter
description: Counters that have no rules meaning, just aesthetic.

name: affinity
description: Affinity is an ability that reduces the mana cost of the spell by the number of permanents of a specified type that the player controls. Variations of this ability exist, such as referencing the number of cards in the players' graveyard or the number of attacking creatures.

name: affinity for domain
description: Affinity reducers that care about the range of land types you control.

name: affinity for land type
description: Affinity abilities that care about land types.

name: affinity for spells
description: Costs 1 less to cast for each instant or sorcery card in your graveyard

name: affinity undergrowth
description: Affinity which cares about how many creatures are in your graveyard.

name: afflict
description: An ability that makes defending players lose life for blocking. It exists primarily as a keyword although variants that mimic the mechanic exists.

name: aikido
description: Effects that turn your opponent's strength against them.

name: alliteration
description: Cards that alliterate, either properly or per Treacherous Trapezist.

name: alt-commander
description: Legendary creatures printed as backup commanders in preconstructed decks, with the same color identity as the face commander.

name: alternate-cost-bounce
description: Cards that let you return permanents to your hand to play them for less/no mana

name: alternate-cost-gain-life
description: Cards that let you give your opponents life to play them for less or no mana

name: alternate-cost-life
description: Cards that let you pay life to play them for less or no mana

name: alternate-cost-sacrifice
description: Cards that let you sacrifice permanents to play them for free/a reduced amount of mana

name: alternate loss condition
description: New ways for you to lose the game

name: alternate win condition
description: New ways for you to win the game (or cause your opponent to lose)

name: alternative crewing
description: Vehicles with crew that also have alternative ways for self-animation. For vehicles without crew see [crewless vehicle](crewless-vehicle).

name: amount spent matters
description: The amount of mana you spent to do the thing matters.

name: anagram
description: Cards whose names are intentional anagrams.

name: animate
description: Effects that turn things into creatures.

name: animate artifact
description: Cards that can turn other noncreature artifacts into creatures. See also [animate vehicle](/tags/card/animate-vehicle).

name: animate enchantment
description: Cards that can turn other noncreature enchantments into creatures.

name: animate land
description: Cards that can turn other noncreature lands into creatures.

name: animate planeswalker
description: Effects that turn planeswalkers into creatures.

name: animate self
description: Noncreatures that can become creatures. For creatures that can stop being creatures, see [deanimate self](deanimate-self).

name: animate vehicle
description: Cards that can turn other vehicles into creatures (other than by crewing them), permanently or temporarily. See also [animate artifact](/tags/card/animate-artifact).

name: ante matters
description: Ante is a depreciated game mechanic where players would remove the top card of their deck from the game and give it to their opponent if they lose. Some early cards in magic would have effects that allowed players to ante more cards for effects.

name: anthem
description: Your entire team gets +N/+N. Possibly limited to creature types or colors.

name: any zone type change
description: These cards change types in every and any zone.

name: arc lightning
description: Deals X damage divided as you choose among one, two, or three targets.

name: arena effect
description: You and an opponent pick a creature you own, then they fight! (Or kiss, in the tunnel of love)

name: armament ability
description: An ability in which the P/T of one or more creatures can increase based on the number of equipment attached to one creature outside of the equipment's effects of being attached. See also [cranial plating](cranial-plating).

name: armoring
description: A toughness-pumping ability. See also [firebreathing] in red and [shade pump] in black.

name: artifactify
description: Effects that turn things into artifacts.

name: artist matters
description: Cards which care about the artist for a card. See also [art matters](/tags/card/art-matters).

name: art matters
description: Cards which care about the contents of art. See also [artist matters](/tags/card/artist-matters).

name: auto buyback
description: Derived from the buyback keyword, this ability will automatically return a card to your hand after casting it usually after a  condition is met.

name: auto equip
description: Equipments that attach themselves to other creatures as they enter the battlefield.

name: awaken
description: Collection tag used to apply the various tags that comprise the keyword Awaken. Should be 1-1 with kw:awaken.

name: balance
description: Effects that attempt to equalize the board state between players by forcing players to sacrifice permanents on the battlefield or discard their cards to a number set by the player with the fewest on the field or in hand.

name: ball lightning
description: A high-damage one-turn creature.

name: banish
description: Exile things as long as this permanent remains.

name: banish-spell
description: Exile a spell on the stack, and bring it back when this permanent goes away.

name: battalion
description: A keyword ability in which an effect triggers when a specific creature attacks with at least two other creatures.

name: battle cry
description: A keyword ability that triggers that whenever a specific creature attacks, each other attacking creature gets +1/+0 until end of turn.

name: bear with set's mechanic
description: A 2/2 for 2 with one of the set's iconic mechanics

name: behold
description: Cards that have an effect if you control something or reveal from your hand a card with a specific attribute.

name: berserk
description: A creature gets a power boost, but will die this turn.

name: bible reference
description: Cards that were originally explicitly based on The Bible

name: birthing pod
description: Derived from the titular card, this refers to the ability that allows to sacrifice a permanent, search your library for another permanent with a converted mana cost either in relation to the sacrificed permanent or the mana you paid, and put it on the battlefield.

name: black effect
description: Effects which are iconically black.

name: block additional
description: Block additional creatures beyond just the one.

name: block unlimited
description: Block an unlimited, or practically unlimited, number of creatures.

name: block without creature
description: These cards make something be blocked ... without anything actually doing the blocking.

name: blood artist ability
description: Cards that have abilities along the lines of: "Whenever creature dies target/each opponent loses 1 life (and you gain 1 life)."

name: blood moon effect
description: Derived from the titular card, this refers to the effect in which lands lose their land type and/or abilities, being replaced by something else.

name: blue effect
description: Effects which are iconically blue.

name: boltland
description: Lands you can pay 3 life/take 3 damage to have them enter untapped.

name: bombard
description: Sacrifice something else to deal N damage. See also [fling](/tags/card/fling), [bombard-self](bombard-self).

name: bombard-self
description: Sacrifice this permanent to deal N damage. See also [fling-self](/tags/card/fling-self), [bombard](bombard).

name: booster tutor
description: Cards, usually acorn cards, that have you open booster packs to add into the game.

name: borrow ability
description: Cards that give themselves or other creatures keyword abilities if something else has it. See also [keyword soup](keyword-soup).

name: bottomless mana sink
description: Repeatable effects that, if an arbitrary amount of mana is dumped into them, will probably win you the game—for example, by drawing your whole deck, creating an arbitrarily large attacker, making an endless army of tokens, gaining infinite life, etc.

name: bounce
description: Returning a permanent from the battlefield to its owner's hand

name: bounceable aura
description: Auras you can bounce back to your hand.

name: bounceland
description: Non-basic lands which tap for two mana, but require you to return a land to your hand when you play them.

name: brainstorm
description: Draw a number of cards, then put cards from your hand on top of your library in any order.

name: breaks-ktk-morph-rule
description: Cards with morph (or a similar ability) which break the Khans of Tarkir block development rule which stated that a morphed creature cannot be turned face-up for less than five mana if doing so would allow it to win combat against a face-down morphed creature.

name: bribery
description: Give an opponent something in exchange for a benefit to you.

name: bring your own crew
description: A vehicle that comes with a built-in pilot to crew it.

name: buff mana
description: Mana that comes with additional benefits when spent.

name: burn
description: Effects that deal damage, whether to creatures, players, or planeswalkers.

name: burn any
description: Cards that burn any target: players, creatures, battles, or planeswalkers.

name: burn-self
description: Creatures deal damage to themselves.

name: burn with set's mechanic
description: Burn spells that have the mechanic of the set they debuted in.

name: burst draw
description: Card draw effects that can net you a bunch of cards at once. As a rule of thumb, to qualify for this tag, the card should let you draw at least three cards at a time and come out ahead by at least +1.

name: bushido
description: Whenever X blocks or becomes blocked, it's power and toughness is modified until end of turn.

name: buttcrew
description: Cards that crew with toughness rather than power.

name: buttfling
description: Fling, but using toughness for damage.

name: buttsaddle
description: Cards that saddle with toughness rather than power.

name: buttstation
description: Cards that station using their toughness rather than their power.

name: buttstrike
description: Creatures assign damage equal to their toughness.

name: cantrip
description: Cards which let you draw a card as they resolve or enter the battlefield

name: card advantage
description: Things that give you access to more cards.  (The technical meaning of card advantage can be much broader than this, but this is what we're using here.)

name: card game reference
description: A series of cards in Mystery Booster 2 that reference other card games.

name: card names
description: A collection of tags identifying properties and naming schemes in cards.

name: cards in exile matter
description: Mechanics that care about the cards in the exile zone.

name: cards in graveyard matter
description: Mechanics that care about the cards in one or more graveyards.

name: card style matters
description: Foiling, alt art, etc matters.

name: castable from exile
description: Cards you can cast or access from exile.

name: castable from graveyard
description: Cards you can cast or access from the graveyard.

name: castable from library
description: Cards you can cast or access from your library.

name: casting restriction
description: Spells with unusual conditions restricting their casting.

name: cast on resolution
description: Spells and abilities that let you cast spells as part of their resolution.

name: cast tax
description: When a spell is cast, something happens unless the player casting the spell pays a cost, usually mana.

name: cast trigger-self
description: Cards that have a trigger when they are cast

name: catalog
description: Draw two cards, then discard a card.

name: catch-22
description: At the end of turn, if a condition isn't met, a consequence is given.

name: catch up
description: Players who have less get more.

name: cda-power
description: Creatures that have their power determined by a characteristic-defining ability.

name: cda-toughness
description: Creatures whose toughness is determined via a characteristic-defining ability.

name: changeling
description: Cards that have all creature types in all zones.

name: change target
description: Cards that change the targets of something on the stack.

name: characteristic-defining ability
description: Cards that have an ability that define something about it that would normally be found somewhere else on the card. Speicifcally, CDAs set color, types, subtypes, power, and/or toughness.

name: charm
description: Modal spells where you pick one option out of three

name: cheaper than mv
description: The actual cost you pay for an effect (or for an alternate mode) will often be lower than the card's mana value.

name: cheat death
description: Return a creature or permanent to the battlefield or to your hand at the moment it dies.

name: clash-like
description: Cards that compare the mana values of multiple players' revealed cards

name: class type only
description: These creatures have only a class type, and no type correlating to whatever fantasy race they might belong to.

name: clone
description: Cards that enter as copies of other things

name: color break
description: Cards known to break the modern color pie, granting a color access to something it shouldn't be able to do.

name: color-choose-land
description: Lands that produce a mana of the color you specified when entering

name: combat arbiter
description: Cards that limit how many can attack and/or block in some way.

name: combat-neutral damage trigger
description: Creature abilities that trigger upon dealing any amount of damage, no matter if it was done in combat or not. Compare and contrast [saboteur](saboteur).

name: combat ramp
description: Generate mana/more lands by attacking and/or dealing combat damage

name: combat timing restriction
description: Spells that can only be cast in combat.

name: combat trick
description: Effects that can be used during combat to help a creature survive, destroy opposing creatures, or deal additional combat damage.

name: command
description: Modal spells where you pick two options out of four

name: commander identity matters
description: Cards that care about your commander's color identity.

name: commander matters
description: Cards that care about commander mechanics. See child tags for the specific mechanics being engaged in.

name: commander set booster cards
description: Card that debuted in a Commander Set but were only obtainable through boosters of the set it was released with.

name: commander tax evasion
description: Don't pay the {2}, become a tax evader! These effects replace, discount, or eliminate commander tax.

name: commander tax matters
description: The extra mana paid to recast your commander matters to these cards.

name: compulsive research
description: Cards with a function of "draw N cards, then discard 2 unless you discard [type]"

name: conditional tapland
description: Lands that will enter the battlefield tapped or untapped depending on certain conditions.

name: cone spell
description: Based on Cone of Flame, these are cards with a theme of doing something three times with the values 1, 2, and 3.

name: confluence
description: Modal spells where you pick three options out of three, with the ability to choose a mode multiple times

name: conjure-artifact
description: Cards that conjure artifacts.

name: conjure-artifact-creature
description: Cards that conjure artifact creatures.

name: conjure-battle
description: Cards that conjure battles.

name: conjure-card
description: Cards that conjure cards of unknown type or of any type of card.

name: conjure-creature
description: Cards that conjure creatures.

name: conjure-duplicate
description: Cards that conjure duplicates instead of named cards or cards from spellbooks.

name: conjure-enchantment
description: Cards that conjure enchantments.

name: conjure-instant
description: Cards that conjure instants.

name: conjure-named
description: Cards that conjure other cards by name.

name: conjure-nonland
description: Cards that conjure nonland cards.

name: conjure-permanent
description: Cards that conjure any type of permanent card.

name: conjure-planeswalker
description: Cards that conjure planeswalkers.

name: conjure-random
description: Cards that conjure random cards.

name: conjure-sorcery
description: Cards that conjure sorceries.

name: conjure-spellbook
description: Cards that conjure cards from spellbooks.

name: conjure-to-battlefield
description: Cards that conjure other cards onto the battlefield.

name: conjure-to-exile
description: Cards that conjure cards into exile.

name: conjure-to-graveyard
description: Cards that conjure cards into graveyards.

name: conjure-to-hand
description: Cards that conjure cards to your hand.

name: conjure-to-library
description: Cards that conjure cards into libraries.

name: consult
description: Dig through your library until you find a card meeting some criteria.

name: consult-cast
description: Consult effects that let you cast the card you find.

name: consult-onto-battlefield
description: Consult effects that put the card you find onto the battlefield.

name: control changing effects
description: Effects that change control of cards, permanents, and spells.

name: converge
description: Spells that do something equal to the number of colors spent to cast them.

name: convoke
description: Cards that let you tap creatures to pay less or no mana for them.

name: copy
description: Effects that copy things

name: copy-legendary
description: These cards can give you a copy of a legendary permanent you control that you can actually keep (instead of immediately sacrificing).

name: copy-nonland
description: Effects that allow you to copy permanents of any type except for land.

name: copy-permanent
description: Effects that allow you to copy any permanent, regardless of its card type.

name: copy-self
description: Cards that make a copy of themselves

name: copy-spell
description: Make a copy of a spell on the stack.

name: copy-token
description: Effects that copy specifically tokens, like populate.

name: cost reducer
description: Cards that make other spells cost less. (Does not apply to effects that reduce a spell's own cost, like delve.)

name: counter fuel
description: Remove counters to do something.

name: counter fuel-aesthetic
description: These cards remove counters with no rules meaning as fuel.

name: counter fuel-any
description: Use any type of counter as fuel.

name: counter fuel-other
description: Use other permanents' counters as fuel.

name: counter fuel-pt
description: These cards remove deprecated P/T counters as fuel.

name: counter preservation-self
description: When this thing leaves, put it's counters on something else you control.

name: counterspell
description: Spells that counter stuff. See child tags for variants on the behaviour; notably [counterspell-soft](/tags/card/counterspell-soft) which gives the opponent a buyout cost.

name: counterspell-ability
description: Cards that counter activated/triggered abilities. NOT for abilities that counter things.

name: counterspell-artifact
description: Counterspells that call out artifacts specifically.

name: counterspell-automatic
description: Cards that will automatically try to counter spells.

name: counterspell-bounce
description: Counterspells that instead "counter" by bouncing the spell.

name: counterspell-creature
description: Counterspells that call out creatures specifically.

name: counterspell-enchantment
description: Counterspells that call out enchantments specifically.

name: counterspell-exile
description: For counterspelling that exiles the spell.

name: counterspell-free
description: Counterspells that can be cast right now with no mana cost (even if they might incur some alternate/later cost).

name: counterspell-instant
description: Counterspells that call out instants specifically.

name: counterspell-noncreature
description: Counterspells that call out noncreatures specifically.

name: counterspell-reusable
description: Counterspell spells and abilities you can use again and again.

name: counterspell-sacrifice
description: Counterspell abilities that require sacrifice as a cost.

name: counterspell-soft
description: A "soft counterspell" gives the other player a chance to buy off the counter, such as with a mana payment.

name: counterspell-sorcery
description: Counterspells that call out sorceries specifically.

name: counterspell-sweeper
description: Counter ALL the things!

name: counterspell-tuck
description: Counter a spell by putting it into its owner's library.

name: counts as a type
description: Cards that used the deprecated "counts as a ..." language in their early prints.

name: cover card
description: Cards that are meant to cover face down cards.

name: cranial plating
description: Buffs based on the number of artifacts or Equipment you control/in play. See also [armament ability](armament-ability). For the enchantment version, see [ethereal armor](ethereal-armor).

name: creates oracle copy
description: These cards create a copy of an Oracle card by name and cast it. (CR 707.13)

name: creates oracle token
description: These cards create a token version of an Oracle card by name (CR 111.11)

name: creates token of a card
description: These cards create a token with characteristics seen on a printed card. There may not be an associated physical token. See the parent tag above for more information.

name: creature-ability-noncreature
description: Noncreature, non-Vehicle, non-Spacecraft permanents with abilities that become more relevant when they're animated as creatures, like combat keywords and attack triggers.

name: creaturefall
description: Cards that care about a creatures entereing the battlefield. Also known as Alliance in New Capenna.

name: creatureland
description: Lands that can animate themselves.

name: creature type name
description: Cards with names comprised of creature types.

name: cunning
description: If X attacks and isn't blocked, you may choose to have it deal damage to another target. If done so, X will deal no combat damage.

name: curiosity
description: Draw cards when dealing damage to an opponent

name: curiosity-like
description: Cards that can give you some form of card advantage upon dealing damage, usually combat damage, to a player.

name: cycle
description: All the cycles from Magic history.

name: cycle-abu-dual-land
description: The original dual lands from Alpha/Beta/Unlimited (ABU).

name: cycle-arch-charm
description: Multi-Set supercycle of cards that cost MMM

name: cycle-blb-hybrid
description: A cycle of 2 colour hybrid creatures from Bloomburrow.

name: cycle-block-grn-guildmage
description: The guildmage cycle from Guilds of Ravnica and Ravnica Allegiance. Each has two activated ability costing one of its colors plus {T}.

name: cycle-block-rav-guildmage
description: The guildmage cycle from the original Ravnica block. They have two activated abilities corresponding to each of their guild's colors.

name: cycle-block-rtr-guildmage
description: The guildmage cycle from Return to Ravnica block. Each has two activated abilities corresponding to both of the guild's colors.

name: cycle-c16-u-monocolor
description: The only monocolored new cards at uncommon from C16.

name: cycle-cmr-backward-partner
description: The backwards half of the draft signpost partner legends in CMR. See also [cycle-cmr-forward-partner](cycle-cmr-forward-partner).

name: cycle-cmr-forward-partner
description: The forward half of the draft signpost partner legends in CMR. See also [cycle-cmr-backward-partner](cycle-cmr-backward-partner).

name: cycle-colorless-land
description: Cycles of lands that produce only colorless mana.

name: cycle-con-forward-synergy
description: Way looser than the backward cycle, I'd say these don't qualify given that there isn't even a green->white card.

name: cycle-cycling-land
description: Cycles of cycling lands. (Alternate title: we saw you like cycles, so we put cycling in your cycles so you can cycle while you cycle.)

name: cycle-dft-team-captain
description: A cycle of legendary creatures featuring the team captains of each team participating in the second edition of the [Ghirapur Grand Prix](https://tagger.scryfall.com/tags/artwork/ghirapur-grand-prix).

name: cycle-dual-land
description: Dual land cycles. See also [dual land](https://tagger.scryfall.com/tags/card/dual-land) for non-cycle dual lands.

name: cycle-ecl-u-behold
description: A cycle of creatures that ask you to behold a creature type or pay {2} as an additional cost.

name: cycle-eld-r-m-two-color
description: The ten two-color rare or mythic rare gold cards found in ELD boosters.

name: cycle-fdn-r-two-color
description: Two-color rares from Foundations found in Play Boosters.

name: cycle-gpt-nephilim
description: The five Nephilim that awoke during Guildpact.

name: cycle-ice-two-color-enemy-hate
description: These allied enchantments hose their common enemy color, either directly or indirectly.

name: cycle-inv-c-nonc-kicker-spell
description: Common noncreature spells from Invasion with a colorless kicker cost.

name: cycle-kld-module
description: The Modules of Kaladesh were designed to work together in this order: Animation -> Decoction -> Fabrication -> repeat.

name: cycle-land
description: All the land cycles. See in particular: [monocolor land cycles](/tags/card/cycle-mono-land), [dual land cycles](/tags/card/cycle-dual-land), and [triland cycles](/tags/card/cycle-triland).

name: cycle-lea-slush-art
description: These cards were designed last-minute for Alpha and not playtested, and designed to use up leftover art. Read more here: [Alpha Top-Down cards  by WotC](https://magic.wizards.com/en/articles/archive/alpha-top-down-cards-2003-06-13)

name: cycle-lrw-lorwyn-five
description: “Lorwyn Five” is a nickname for the five original planeswalker cards that debuted in Lorwyn.

name: cycle-m11-c-pw-signature
description: See also [cycle-m11-u-pw-signature](/tags/card/cycle-m11-u-pw-signature)

name: cycle-m11-u-pw-signature
description: See also [cycle-m11-c-pw-signature](/tags/card/cycle-m11-c-pw-signature)

name: cycle-m20-doubles
description: Five cards with alliterative names and a theme of doing something twice.

name: cycle-mir-fetchland
description: Also known as "slow fetches", a cycle of lands from mirage that enter tapped and can be sacrificed to tutor a land that is one of two specific types to the battlefield

name: cycle-mmq-cateran-recruiter
description: A double vertical cycle of six Mercenaires from Mercadian Masques. Each one has the Mercenary recruiting ability  with converted mana costs from 1 to 6.

name: cycle-mmq-ramosian-recruiter
description: A cycle of five Rebels from Mercadian Masques. Each one has the Rebel recruiting ability searching for converted mana costs from 2 to 6.

name: cycle-mono-land
description: Monocolor land cycles.

name: cycle-morphling
description: The "true" cycle of morphlings. See also [morphling](morphling) for similar cards.

name: cycle-mrd-artifact-land
description: The artifact lands from the Mirrodin block.

name: cycle-nonmana-land
description: Cycles of lands that don't create mana.

name: cycle-ody-filterland
description: The cycle of filterlands originally debuting with just the ally color pairs in Odyssey and eventually completed in the Fallout commander decks.

name: cycle-ody-restock-creature
description: A vertical cycle of creatures from Torment that restock.

name: cycle-ody-wincon-enchantment
description: The cycle of five alternate win condition enchantments from Odyssey block.

name: cycle-ody-x-of-the-y
description: A vertical cycle of token-producing sorceries with flashback from Odyssey.

name: cycle-plc-tsb-colorshift
description: These cards are colorshifted versions of various cards [reprinted in the Time Spiral Timeshifted set](https://scryfall.com/search?q=s%3Atsb+%28tsh+or+lch+or+gde+or+lso+or+ndb%29&order=color).

name: cycle-rav-guild-artifact
description: The unique iconic artifacts for each guild from the original Ravnica block.

name: cycle-rav-shockland
description: The original cycle of shocklands from Ravnica block.

name: cycle-reveal-land
description: Dual lands that enter the battlefield untapped if you reveal a card with a basic land type that matches a color they tap for.

name: cycle-rix-immortal-sun-purpose
description: A cycle of cards from Rivals of Ixalan that had flavor text representing the Immortal Sun's importance to each of the factions.

name: cycle-shm-hideaway-creature
description: These creatures are the ones hidden inside the hideaway lands.

name: cycle-snc-r-tricolor
description: The cycle of three-color rares from SNC.

name: cycle-sword-ally-color
description: The cycle of ally-colored Swords of X and Y, begun in Modern Horizons.

name: cycle-sword-enemy-color
description: The cycle of enemy-colored Swords of X and Y.

name: cycle-tangoland
description: Lands that enter tapped unless you control two or more basic lands. Named after the phrase "two to tango"

name: cycle-tmc-m-turtle
description: The four monocolor face legends of the Turtle Team-Up decks.

name: cycle-tmc-teamup
description: A four-card cycle pairing each Ninja Turtle with one of the team's non-turtle allies.

name: cycle-tor-black-dreams
description: A vertical cycle of black Dreams cards from Torment.

name: cycle-tor-red-punisher
description: A vertical cycle of red punisher cards from Torment.

name: cycle-triland
description: Triland cycles. See also [triland](/tags/card/triland/) for non-cycle trilands.

name: cycle-tr-mage
description: A multi-set "mega cycle" of wizards that tutor for an artifact card of a specific mana value, with their card name starting with "Tr-"

name: cycle-tsp-no-mana-cost-suspend
description: A cycle of older cards, but now with suspend. (And no mana cost, so you have to use suspend)

name: cycle-ulg-creatureland
description: The Urza's Legacy monocolored creaturelands

name: cycle-und-enemy-colored-legend
description: Legends from Unsanctioned with an enemy-colored activated ability.

name: cycle-ust-flavor-variant
description: Cards in Unstable that have multiple versions with the same collector number, same name, same mana cost, and same rules text, but different art and/or flavor text.

name: cycle-ust-functional-variant
description: Cards in Unstable that have multiple versions with the same collector number, but different mana cost and/or rules text.

name: cycle-verge
description: Dual lands that allows you to tap for one color with no restrictions, but only allow you to tap for the other color if you have a land of one of two basic land types .

name: cycle-zendikar-creatureland
description: The supercycle of dual creaturelands, begun in Worldwake and completed in Battle for Zendikar.

name: cycle-zen-fetchland
description: The Enemy colored counterparts to the Onslaught Fetch cycle.

name: cycle-znr-multiclass-creature
description: A vertical cycle of creatures that were every Party mechanic class.

name: damage increaser
description: Things deal +N damage. See also [damage multiplier](/tags/card/damage-multiplier).

name: damage multiplier
description: Things deal double or triple damage. See also [damage increaser](/tags/card/damage-increaser).

name: damage stays
description: Cards that prevent damage from being removed from one or more creatures during cleanup steps.

name: daunt
description: The "can't be blocked by power 2 or less" as named by WotC. See also [gives daunt](gives-daunt).

name: day/night
description: Effects that make use of or interact with the Day/Night mechanic introduced in Innistrad: Midnight Hunt, including but not limited to cards with the Daybound and Nightbound keywords.

name: day zero errata
description: These cards received errata before or immediately after their initial release.

name: deal with the devil
description: Black enchantments that come with a major drawback that could doom you. See also [Lich effect](/tags/card/lich-effect)

name: deanimate
description: Uncreaturize a creature.

name: deanimate self
description: Creatures that can stop being creatures. For noncreatures that may become creatures, see [animate self](animate-self).

name: deathtouch to planeswalkers
description: Abilities that destroy planeswalkers that receive damage.

name: death trigger
description: Abilities that trigger from permanents being sent to a graveyard.

name: death trigger opponent
description: Cards that trigger when an opponent's permanent is sent to a graveyard.

name: death trigger-self
description: Permanents with abilities that trigger when they die.

name: deck requirement
description: Cards that require you to have a deck with specific attributes, or with an effect that requires you to have your deck be built with specific attributes.

name: defector
description: Permanents that move around the board on their own

name: delayed cantrip
description: Draw a card next turn.

name: delayed payment
description: Cards which you can play now, and pay for later

name: delayed trigger
description: Create a triggered ability that may trigger later. Similar to a [reflexive trigger](reflexive-trigger).

name: delve
description: Exile cards from your graveyard to reduce the cost

name: demilich effect
description: Exiles cards from graveyard, but lets you cast copies of them. Essentially (usually) one-time recursion

name: deprecated card types
description: Cards featuring deprecated card types in some of their prints.

name: deprecated legend restriction
description: Until Champions of Kamigawa introduced the Legendary Supertype, the Legend rule was built into the creature type, meaning that certain cards would bring it up in the rules for their abilities.

name: deprecated legend type
description: These cards were originally printed with the Legend creature type. Their type line read “Summon Legend” or “Creature — Legend”.

name: deprecated mechanics
description: Mechanics that have been deprecated by newer developments.

name: deprecated p/t counter
description: In the early days Magic used all kinds of power/toughness modifying counters. Nowadays it just sticks with +1/+1 and -1/-1 counters.

name: deprecated untapped artifact
description: Until 6th edition, artifacts were deactivated when they were tapped. These cards from prior to 6ed have that behaviour baked into their current rules text.

name: deprecated wall restriction
description: Until Champions of Kamigawa introduced defender, the Wall subtype would make a creature unable to attack. As such, certain cards used to exempt Walls for balance reasons.

name: detain
description: Prevent a creature from attacking, blocking, or using it's activated abilities until one's next turn.

name: devour
description: Before a creature enters the battlefield, the player may sacrifice a number of permanents and the creature will enter with +1/+1 counters on it in relation to the number of permanents sacrificed.

name: dexterity
description: Cards that  require physical interaction outside of normal game scope, such as tossing cards or specific usage of one's head or hands. This can be an accessibility issue for some who have limited mobility.

name: dice roll
description: Cards that involve rolling dice.

name: differently named lands matter
description: These cards want you to have multiple lands with different names.

name: digital-only mechanics
description: Cards with mechanics designed for the digital environment of Magic Arena. Includes Unknown and acorn cards with mechanics equivalent to an existing Arena one.

name: digital to paper
description: These cards were originally created for a set that did not see a physical printing, but were later printed in paper.

name: dilemma
description: Effects that give you two equally bad choices.

name: discard
description: Gets cards out of your opponent's hand and into their graveyard.

name: discarded type matters
description: The type of card you discard matters for some effect.

name: discard to exile
description: Put cards from the opponent's hand into exile, either permanently or by [banishing](banish-hand) them.

name: discard to library
description: Put cards from the opponent's hand back into their library.

name: discount-self
description: Cards that have a way to reduce their own cost

name: disenchant/naturalize
description: Cards that remove artifacts and/or enchantments.

name: disintegrate
description: Damaged creatures are exiled instead of dying.

name: distinct echo cost
description: Cards where the echo cost is different from the casting cost

name: ditch hand
description: Cards that let you get rid of your current hand in some way or other.

name: division
description:
Cards that ask for a divided value.
For cards that include a fractional value or a number that is not a whole number, see [non-integer](non-integer).

name: divvy
description: Cards that make you choose from two or more piles.

name: dnd
description: Cards made to reference mechanical parts of the Dungeons and Dragons TRPG.

name: dnd book
description: Cards that are named after an actual printed Dungeons and Dragons book or adventure module.

name: dnd character
description: Cards that represent Dungeons and Dragons characters: name, rules text, etc. See also the [dungeons and dragons](/tags/artwork/dungeons-and-dragons) art tag.

name: dnd item
description: Cards named after and representing DnD items.

name: dnd mechanic
description: Cards named after and representing DnD mechanics.

name: dnd monster
description: Cards named after and representing monsters from DnD.

name: dnd spell
description: Cards originally from DnD sets that are named after and representing DnD spells.

name: doctor who episode saga
description: A series of cards named after and mechanically referencing various doctor who episodes.

name: doesn't untap
description: Cards that prevent their own untapping in some way.

name: dolmen ability
description: Derived from the titular card, this ability prevents all combat damage that would be dealt to attacking creatures you control. Other conditions may apply.

name: donate
description: Effects that give control of a card you control to another player

name: donate rampant growth
description: Cards that allow you to ramp another player's mana by putting a land from their deck onto the battlefield.

name: donate token
description: Effects that can create tokens under an opponents control.

name: doom blade
description: 2 MV "Destroy target creature, unless it's X"

name: draft matters
description: Cards that care about their use in limited formats in some way

name: draft signpost
description: Cards that represent a draft archetype in a given set. Typically designed as ten two-color uncommons (2CUDS).

name: drain creature
description: Hurt a creature and gain life to match.

name: drain life
description: Hurt your opponent and gain life to match.

name: drain strength
description: One thing gets bigger, another gets smaller.

name: draw
description: Spells and abilities that textually draw you cards. See also [pure draw](pure-draw).

name: drawback
description: Cards that have some kind of disadvantage.

name: draw engine
description: Card draw effects that can net you additional cards repeatedly over time. Contrast [Burst Draw](burst-draw), which nets you multiple cards right away.

name: drawlink
description: Effects that cause combat damage dealt to draw that many cards.

name: earthbend
description: Collection tag used to apply the various tags that comprise the keyword Earthbend. Should be 1:1 with kw:earthbend.

name: earthquake
description: Spells that deal damage to creatures without flying.

name: eat food
description: Cards that involve actual food, generally being eaten.

name: egg
description: Artifacts that have to be sacrificed to do something.

name: emblem-lite
description: Effects that work like emblems, but aren't emblems.

name: emerge
description: Creatures that let you sacrifice something to cast them at a cost reduced by the mana cost of the creature sacrificed

name: emerge-from-artifact
description: Cards where you can sacrifice an artifact and subtract its cost from the mana you pay

name: emerge-from-creature
description: Cards where you can sacrifice a creature and subtract its cost from the mana you pay

name: eminence
description: An ability that works either in the command zone or on the battlefield, introduced by the tribal leaders of Commander 2017.

name: empty library
description: Cards that respond to an empty library.

name: enchantment engine
description: Whenever you cast an enchantment spell, draw a card.

name: enchantmentfall
description: Cards that care about enchantments entering the batllefield.

name: enchantmentize
description: Effects that turn things into enchantments.

name: energy generator
description: Cards that in some way give you energy counters.

name: energy increaser
description: Cards that increase the amount of energy you get.

name: enlarge
description: Effects that give a creature +N/+M. One of these values should be significant, at least 3.

name: enrage
description: An effect that triggers when the creature (or attached/enchanted creature) takes damage.

name: enters-and-leaves-trigger-self
description: Permanents that have an effect that triggers when the permanent enters the battlefield or leaves the battlefield to any zone.

name: enters in company
description: Creatures that come with more creatures attached on ETB without reanimating them.

name: eponymous
description: Non-token cards with a name matching their subtypes.

name: eponymous planeswalker
description: Planeswalker cards that are just the full names of the character in question

name: equipless equipment
description: Equipment that do not have an "Equip – [Cost]" ability printed on them.

name: ethereal armor
description: Buffs based on the number of enchantments or Auras you control/in play. See also [Uril ability](uril-ability). For the artifact version, see [cranial plating](cranial-plating).

name: evasion
description: Creatures that are harder to block through keywords (flying, menace) or other means.

name: exalted
description: Creatures attacking alone get +N/+N

name: exhaust
description: Abilities that you can only activate once.

name: exile on resolution
description: Cards that exile themselves once they've finished all their effects. Commonly seen on extra turn cards.

name: exile-self
description: Cards which exile themselves (from the battlefield, from the graveyard, from your hand, etc.)

name: exile-self-dfc-transform
description: DFCs that exile themselves as part of their transformation.

name: exile with tax
description: Exile something, but let them pay to get it back.

name: expertise
description: Effects that allow you to cast a card with a given mana value or less from your hand without paying its mana cost, usually in association with another effect.

name: exponential
description: Effects that involve some amount of exponential growth. This can be an exponential value, or a token creation effect that would be exponential if allowed to progress unhindered. See [quadratic](quadratic) for a different, but similar kind of effect.

name: external prop
description: Cards that involve external props, whether objects or body parts, other than the standard tokens.

name: extract
description: Exile cards from a library. They're just gone now.

name: extra draft
description: Draft additional cards outside the normal bounds of drafting.

name: extra land
description: Effects that allow you to put additional lands from your hand, top of the library, or graveyard onto the battlefield outside of your normal once-per-turn land play. For effects that let you search your library for cards, refer to [tutor land](/tags/card/tutor-land).

name: fact or fiction
description: Pile/dilemma based card advantage. An opponent is involved in choosing or making the piles.

name: fake flying
description: Creatures without flying that can't be blocked except by creatures with flying or reach. See also [gives fake flying](gives-fake-flying).

name: fateseal
description: The ability to 'scry' another player's library instead of your own. Variants of this ability may allow you to choose between your own library and that of another player.

name: faux targeting
description: Things that have to choose "targets", but don't actually *target*, meaning this bypasses keywords such as hexproof, protection, and shroud.

name: ferocious
description: Cards that care about you controlling creatures with power 4 or greater.

name: fetchland
description: Lands that sacrifice to tutor for other lands

name: filterland
description: Lands that take one mana and give you back another colour of mana, sometimes in greater quantities.

name: firebreathing
description: Give a creature +X/+0 until end of turn via an activated ability.

name: fire drake
description: A small family of drakes with once-only firebreathing abilities.

name: flavor matters
description: Not to be confused with [flavor text matters](/tags/card/flavor-text-matters)

name: flavors of vanilla
description: Magic has various flavors of "vanilla", referencing cards with no abilities or only keyword abilities. For vanilla creatures (those with no abilities) see [is:vanilla](https://scryfall.com/search?q=is%3Avanilla) in Scryfall search.

name: flicker
description: Exile a card then return it to the battlefield (now or soon).

name: flicker-nonland
description: Flicker effects that work on all permanent types other than land.

name: flicker-permanent
description: Flicker effects that work on all permanent types: artifact, creature, enchantment, land, planeswalker, and battle.

name: flicker-slow
description: Exile a card to be brought back after a set time (usually at end of turn).

name: fling
description: Sacrifice a creature to deal its power in damage to something. See also [bombard](/tags/card/bombard), [one-sided fight](/tags/card/one-sided-fight), [fling-self](fling-self).

name: fling-self
description: Sacrifice this creature to deal its power in damage. See also [bombard-self](/tags/card/bombard-self), [fling](fling).

name: flowstone
description: Abilities that grant +N/-N and/or -N/+N

name: fog
description: Effects that can prevent all or most of the damage from an entire combat, similar to the card Fog. See also [pseudo-fog](https://tagger.scryfall.com/tags/card/pseudo-fog).

name: fog-selective
description: Fog effects where the damage prevention is or has the potential to be asymmetrical in some way, usually by exempting a subset of attackers/defenders.

name: forced attacker
description: Creatures that have to attack every turn if they can

name: force draw
description: Force an opponent to draw cards, whether they like it or not. The lesser used mode on Ancestral Recall.

name: form
description: Cards with the intent of "transforming you" into something, usually involving various benefits and drawbacks.

name: formidable
description: Effects that care if you control creatures with a total power 8 or greater.

name: four plus creature types
description: Non-Changeling creatures that have four or more creature types

name: fractional life/damage
description: Cards that dealt ½ damage or gained ½ life from Unhinged

name: fractional power/toughness
description: Cards that have ½ power/toughness, or grant it

name: free-cast-another
description: Cast other things without paying their mana cost.

name: free discard outlet
description: Ways to discard repeatedly that require no additional cost or numerical limit.

name: free sacrifice outlet
description: Ways to sacrifice a permanent repeatedly that require no additional cost.

name: freeze
description: A permanent doesn't untap during its next N untap steps. For effects that prevent untapping indefinitely, see [lockdown](lockdown).

name: freeze-creature
description: Prevent a creature from untapping for its next N untaps. For effects that prevent untapping indefinitely, see [lockdown](lockdown-creature).

name: freeze-permanent-any
description: Freeze/stun effects that work against permanents of all types.

name: french vanilla
description: These creatures, vehicles and spacecraft have only keyword abilities.

name: french vanilla equipment
description: Equipment that give a creature a stat change and/or a keyword with no other effects.

name: french vanilla walker
description: French vanilla creatures with only a landwalk ability.

name: frost armor
description: Spells cost additional mana to target stuff you control.

name: fulfilled futureshift
description: This card was initially futureshifted, and has now received its proper print in its future expansion.

name: functional art
description: Un-Set cards in which their art doubles as a function of the card, usually as a way to track things.

name: functional reminder counter
description: Arbitrarily long effects tracked with a counter, such that removing the counter functionally changes the tracked effect. See also [nonfunctional reminder counter](/tags/card/nonfunctional-reminder-counter).

name: fun ruling
description: Cards with rulings where the rules manager is having fun with us. Not necessarily laugh-out-loud funny.

name: future sight engine
description: Cards that reveal the top card of your library and let you play from it. See also [precognition engine](/tags/card/precognition-engine) for a more recent variant in this vein.

name: gainland
description: Lands that have you gain life when they enter the battlefield.

name: gamble
description: Search for a card, and then discard a card at random (with a chance to discard the card you tutored for)

name: game name
description: Cards that are named after game terms.

name: genesis effect
description: Effects that put creatures onto the battlefield from near the top of your library.

name: giant growth
description: Combat tricks that give a creature +N/+N. See also [Enlarge](enlarge).

name: gifts ungiven
description: Get X cards from a location, opponent picks an amount of those you get to keep.

name: gives castable from graveyard
description: Allows you to play other cards from the graveyard.

name: gives castable from nonhand
description: Allows you to play other cards from zones other than your hand.

name: gives changeling
description: Granting the changeling ability doesn't actually do anything in the layer system, so these cards grant all creature types.

name: gives evasion
description: Cards that make a creature harder or impossible to block.

name: gives fake flying
description: Cards that make creatures unblockable except by creatures with flying or reach without giving them flying, known as [fake flying](fake-flying).

name: gives flashback
description: Gives the textual flashback effect. For cards that let you cast others out of the graveyard in general, check [Gives castable from graveyard](gives-castable-from-graveyard)

name: gives high flying
description: These cards give other creatures [high flying](high-flying).

name: gives poisonous
description: Gives a creature the ability to give a set amount of poison counters to opponents upon combat damage, like the poisonous keyword

name: gives pp counters
description: Cards that give +1/+1 counters

name: gives pp counters to all
description: Put +1/+1 counters on all of your creatures.

name: gives stalking
description: Cards that make creatures only be blockable by no more than one creature, also known as "[stalking](stalking)".

name: gives unstoppable
description: Make your creatures assign combat damage as though they weren't blocked. See also [unstoppable](unstoppable).

name: grave pact
description: Cards that have abilities worded: "whenever creature you contol dies, each opponent sacrifices a creature."

name: graveyard fuel
description: Cards that require exiling other cards in graveyard as a cost for abilities and effects.

name: graveyard fuel-creature
description: Exiles creatures from the graveyard for value

name: graveyard order matters
description: These cards care about the order of cards in a graveyard, or rearrange them somehow. If any of these cards are in a player's deck, care must be taken to preserve the order of the graveyard.

name: graveyard seal
description: Prevent interaction with the graveyard or reanimation, or make it more difficult.

name: great-designer-search-3
description: Cards that are directly from or seemenly inspired by card designs from The Great Designer Search 3, a competition to find new magic designers.

name: grim return
description: Get stuff that just went away back on the battlefield.

name: grind
description: Mill cards until N land cards are milled.

name: group hug
description: Cards that can be used to benefit other players, including opponents, usually by giving them resources

name: group slug
description: Cards that are able to deal damage or otherwise target multiple players at once

name: guess
description: Effects that force you to guess at unknown information.

name: guest designer
description: Cards created by an external guest designer.

name: gunk
description: A proposed mechanic designed by Richard Garfield: Putting "cards" into an opponent's library.

name: half mana
description: Cards that add or use one-half mana from Unhinged.

name: hand disruption
description: Mess with your opponent's hand.

name: hand-negative
description: Spell and abilities that leave you with less cards in hand after resolving.

name: hand-neutral
description: Spell and abilities that leave you with the same amount of cards in hand after resolving.

name: hand-positive
description: Spells and abilities that leave you with more cards in hand after resolving.

name: hand size increase
description: Cards that increase the maximum amount of cards you can hold.

name: harmonic
description: Cards that care about artifacts and enchantments separately.

name: has identical token
description: A token exists that's identical to these cards, except for CMC. See more: [token versions of cards](/tags/card/token-versions-of-cards).

name: hate
description: Cards that hate on things — colors, card types, zones, etc.

name: hatebear
description: Creatures that are low-cost (2 MV or less) and have low-power/toughness with relevant effects that may disrupt an opponent's strategy. These are traditionally associated with white.

name: hatebird
description: (Usually) 3 MV flying creatures that mirror the lower-costed [hatebears](hatebear).

name: hate-color
description: Color hate for one or more colors. See also [hate-colorless](/tags/card/hate-colorless).

name: hate-color-choose
description: Color hate where you choose a color to hate on.

name: hate-colored
description: Color hate for cards that are any color at all.

name: hate-colorless
description: Hate on colorless cards. See also [hate-color](/tags/card/hate-color) for color hate.

name: hate-discard
description: Your opponent had better think twice before targeting you with that [Mind Rot](https://tagger.scryfall.com/card/ori/281)!

name: hate empty hand
description: Cards that punish opponents for having few or no cards in hand. For cards that care about their controller's hand being empty, see [hellbent](hellbent) and [heckbent](heckbent).

name: hate-etb
description: Disrupts enters-the-battlefield triggers

name: hate full hand
description: Cards that punish an opponent for having lots of cards in hand.

name: hate-high-pt
description: Cards that specifically hate on cards with high power or toughness, typically at least 3-4.

name: hate-named
description: Hate a specific card of your choice. Yes, that one.

name: hate-planeswalker
description: Effects that specifically answer or are better against planeswalkers. This does not include effects that happen to trigger whether its a player or planeswalker and don't help remove the planeswalker.

name: hate-set-mechanic
description: Cards that "hate" on one or more mechanics in the set they were printed in.

name: hate-typal
description: Hates out one or more creature types.

name: hate-wide
description: The more creatures your opponent controls, the more these cards scale up to punish them for it.

name: hats matter
description: Cards that care about hats appearing in card art.

name: haven
description: Exile your permanents to return them at much later point, essentially storing them somewhere safe. Not to be confused with [flicker](/tags/card/flicker).

name: heckbent
description: Effects that care about having one or fewer cards in hand.

name: hellbending
description: Cards that let you go hellbent, usually by letting you empty your entire hand.

name: hellbent
description: Effects that care about having no cards in hand.

name: helper card
description: Cards printed to assist you with using mechanics.

name: heroic
description: Whenever you cast a spell targeting your permanent, something good happens.

name: hexproof soft
description: Stuff is harder to target, but not impossible.

name: high flying
description: Flying creatures that can only block other flying creatures. See also [gives high flying](gives-high-flying).

name: homeward effect
description: Regain control of stuff you own.

name: humble
description: Nerf a creature's stats and/or abilities as a form of removal. See also [shapechange](/tags/card/shapechange), [swap removal](swap-removal) and [deanimate](deanimate).

name: hunger
description: Creatures dying to this one make this one grow stronger. Coined by Maro: http://markrosewater.tumblr.com/post/178364542148

name: hunger reanimation
description: Creatures this one kills come back into play under your control.

name: hunger trigger
description: Abilities that trigger off other creatures dying to this one. Named after the more-specific Hunger ability.

name: hungry demon
description: Cards that make you sacrifice a creature, either automatically or with a downside if you can't or won't.

name: hurricane
description: Spells that deal damage to creatures with flying. See [earthquake] for the inverse effect.

name: illusion ability
description: Permanents that sacrifice themselves if they get targeted by anything

name: impact effect
description: Effects that have your creatures do ETB damage or life loss.

name: impulse
description: Look at the top N cards and put 1+ of them into your hand. Not to be confused with Red's pseudo-card-draw effect, [impulsive draw](/tags/card/impulsive-draw).

name: impulsive curiosity
description: Exile your cards when a creature deals damage to an opponent, and you can play them for a limited duration.

name: impulsive draw
description: Cards which let you cast a card for a limited time off the top of a library (via exile). Use it or lose it. Typically a red card advantage mechanic. For the effect seen on the [card Impulse](/card/vis/34), see the [tag impulse](/tags/card/impulse).

name: impulsive recursion
description: Sends cards to exile from the graveyard and you can cast them for a limited time. Similar to [Impulsive Draw](/tags/card/impulsive-draw), but from the graveyard, not the library.

name: indefinite effect
description: Effects that last forever, sometimes tracked, sometimes untracked.

name: ingest
description: Permanents that exile one or more cards from the top of an opponent's library upon dealing combat damage.

name: inscryption achievement
description: Cards that share a name with achievements in the PC game Inscryption

name: instant-sorcery-dichotomous
description: Cards that care about instants and sorceries separately.

name: interrupt
description: This spell was an interrupt before Sixth Edition Rules introduced the stack.

name: intervening if clause
description: At/When/Whenever [event], if [clause], [effect].

name: invitational card
description: Cards designed by players who won Invitationals. The original print traditionally features the winning player. See also [Player Spotlight](player-spotlight).

name: jump
description: A creature gains flying until end of turn.

name: karnstructs
description: Cards that make the same construct tokens as Karn, Scion of Urza.

name: keyword counter
description: A collection of all the keyword counters referenced by cards.

name: keyword errata
description: These cards originally spelled out an ability that became a keyword, and were thus given errata.

name: keyword errata scry
description: Cards that Scry but were either printed before the keyword was made, or before it was made deciduous in Magic Origins

name: keyword errata surveil
description: Cards that Surveil but were either printed before the keyword was made, or before it was made deciduous in The Brothers' War

name: keyword soup
description: These cards list out all or almost all the keyword abilities found in their set, possibly restricted by color.

name: kismet effect
description: Cards that make certain types of permanents enter the battlefield tapped.

name: land conversion
description: Effects that change or add land types and affect how lands produce mana.

name: landfall
description: Cards which reward you for playing lands.

name: landfall other
description: Effects that trigger when lands enter the battlefield under an opponent's control.

name: landhome
description: An old two-part mechanic: you had to control a land of a certain type, and so did the opponent. It often got used in just one of the halves, so we tag either half.

name: landslow
description: Cards that prevent or otherwise limit the lands one can play

name: last chance
description: Red cards that manipulate the turn cycle, then kill you.

name: leaves battlefield trigger
description: Cards that trigger on something leaving the battlefield.

name: legacy
description: Effects that involve physical alteration or selection of the card itself before or during the game.

name: legendary team-up
description: March of the Machines introduced a number of cards representing legendary cards teaming up. (And some playtest cards expanded on the concept.)

name: legendfall
description: Cards that care about legendaries entering the battlefield.

name: legends retold
description: A series of Set Booster-exclusives released in Dominaria United, all of which are reworks of some of the original legendary creatures from Legends.

name: leyline
description: Cards that start the game on the battlefield if they're in your opening hand.

name: lhurgoyf
description: Cards with power and/or toughness equal to number of cards in the graveyard, or a similar variant. This term is referenced from the Mechanical Color Pie 2017 article by Mark Rosewater. See also [maro](maro) and [warlord](warlord).

name: lich effect
description: Effects which turn damage into the loss of cards or permanents. See also [Deal with the devil](/tags/card/deal-with-the-devil)

name: life-and-death-trigger-self
description: Creatures that have an effect that triggers when they enter the battlefield and when they die.

name: life divider
description: Effects that make players lose fractions of their life total. See also [set life total](/tags/card/set-life-total) and [life doubler](/tags/card/life-doubler).

name: life doubler
description: Double someone's life. See also [set life total](/tags/card/set-life-total) and [life divider](/tags/card/life-divider).

name: life for cards
description: Get cards in exchange for your life (damage causes loss of life).

name: lifegain-to-damage
description: Transforms player lifegain into player damage.

name: life payment
description: Cards that cost life to use; not necessarily with the "pay" keyword.

name: life-total-matters-self
description: Cards that care about your life total being above or below a certain amount. This doesn’t include cards like Phyrexian Arena that simply want you to be alive.

name: lightning bolt redux
description: These cards deal 3 damage for {R} under the right circumstances, like Lightning Bolt.

name: living weapon
description: Equipments that attach themselves to a creature they create on ETB.

name: lobotomy
description: Exile all cards of a particular name from a player's hand, library, and/or graveyard. See also [nevermore](/tags/card/nevermore), which forbids certain cards from being cast.

name: lockdown
description: A permanent stays tapped. Named in the Color Pie 2017 article. See also [freeze](freeze).

name: loner
description: Cards that want you to have only one creature, or creatures that want to be the only creature a player controls

name: loot
description: Draw a card, then discard a card. Mainly blue. See also [rummage](/tags/card/rummage), the red version, which discards then draws.

name: loot to library
description: Draw, then shuffle cards into your library or put them on the bottom of it. Contrast [brainstorm](brainstorm).

name: lose trigger
description: Cards that trigger when an opponent loses the game (as opposed a replacement effect for yourself).

name: lure
description: All creatures able to block this creature must do so. See also [lure-limited] and [provoke lite].

name: lure-limited
description: This creature must be blocked, but not necessarily by all creatures like a true lure.

name: madness
description: The specific keyword, madness (Used for applying specific subtags)

name: magic term name
description: Card names that are specifically terms used by Wizards of the Coast or fans of Magic: The Gathering

name: mana ability with extra effect
description: Mana abilities that have some extra effect upon activating them, meaning that they can do actions in strange cases such as during the casting of a split second card and usually can't be prevented in any way.

name: mana cost matters
description: Cards that care about the actual symbols in a mana cost.

name: mana dork
description: Low-cost creatures which can repeatedly generate mana

name: mana dork egg
description: Creatures that can be sacrificed to add mana.

name: mana egg
description: Mana rocks that have to be sacrificed to add mana. They may ramp or merely filter your mana.

name: mana filter
description: Convert mana to other colors.

name: mana fix
description: Allows your lands to tap for additional color(s) of mana. See also [mana filter](https://tagger.scryfall.com/tags/card/mana-filter), [land conversion](https://tagger.scryfall.com/tags/card/land-conversion).

name: mana gorger
description: Cards that grow bigger when players cast spells.

name: mana increaser
description: Cards that increase the mana produced by mana producers, usually lands.

name: mana leak series
description: Soft counterspells with CMC N that cost > N.

name: manaless land
description: Lands that don't have any way to tap for mana. Typically considered a depreciated mechanic besides lands that are meant to search for other lands that tap for mana.

name: manaless value
description: Cards that can provide some amount of value without untapped lands.

name: mana producer
description: non-land cards which generate mana

name: mana restriction
description: Spells with unusual restrictions on how they can be paid for.

name: mana rock
description: Noncreature artifacts that net increase your mana.

name: mana sink
description: Cards with repeatable effects that you can dump a bunch of mana (at least 3) into each turn.

name: mana source (type)
description: These cards had the deprecated mana source type during some of their prints.

name: mana spent matters
description: Effects that care about the amount, type, and/or qualities of mana spent to do a thing.

name: mana storage
description: Cards that can be used to store mana for later phases and/or turns. The storing methods are varied—many use counters to count stored mana, while others create tokens.

name: man-o'-war
description: Creatures that can bounce your opponent's stuff when they enter the battlefield.

name: marauder
description: Enters and causes all players to sacrifice a creature.

name: maro
description: Cards with P/T equal to cards in hand. Named after the card Maro, a nickname for Mark Rosewater, the head designer for Magic: The Gathering.

name: maro-sorcerer
description: Creatures that scale in power and toughness with the number of lands you control.

name: martyr
description: Creatures that sacrifice themselves for benefit.

name: mass land denial
description: Effects that regularly destroy, exile, and bounce other lands, keep lands tapped, or change what mana is produced by four or more lands per player without replacing them.

name: mass reanimation
description: Reanimate everything (or every thing of a kind) from one or more graveyards (or potentially do this).

name: mass shrink
description: Shrink your opponent's whole board (or part of it).

name: mechanical foreshadow
description: Cards that refer to another card, type, or mechanic not yet seen in the game.

name: melded
description: Melded permanents made by the combined back faces of two meld cards.

name: meme
description: Cards that have become memes in Magic, or are explicitly based off of memes.

name: metalcraft
description: Effects when the player controls three or more artifacts.

name: mill
description: A keyword action used in Magic to describe the action of a player taking cards from the top of their library and putting them into their graveyard.

name: mill-any
description: Cards that let you choose a library to mill. Could be yours, could be theirs. It could also be an indirect choice, e.g. milling the controller of a targeted permanent.

name: mill-each
description: Cards that mill each player simultaneously, putting cards from top of their library to their graveyards.

name: mill-exile
description: Exile cards from the top of a library.

name: mill-self
description: Cards that put cards from top of your own library to the graveyard. Check the tag "mill" for more.

name: mimic
description: Cards that can copy (mimic) the abilities of other cards.

name: minigame
description: Engages one or more other players in a bet or mind game (not necessarily explicitly).

name: miniwheel
description: Discard your hand, and draw less than seven cards. See also [wheel](/tags/card/wheel) for drawing (potentially) seven or more cards.

name: mirrored knight
description: Magic has a history of having pairs of knights oppose each other.

name: mirror-gallery
description: Cards that void the legendary rule, allowing you to have multiple copies of legendary permanents on the battlefield

name: misnomer
description: Cards with names that imply something that card isn't, such as a mechanic or a color.

name: mix-and-match
description: Two or more non-evergreen mechanics blended together on the same card.

name: mixed subtypes
description: These cards have subtypes on the same type line that belong to different card types. (For example, Dryad is a creature subtype and Forest is a land subtype.)

name: mm counter cost
description: Cards that put -1/-1 counters on your stuff as a cost or requirement for other effects.

name: mm counters matter
description: Cards that care about -1/-1 counters in some way.

name: modal
description: Spells which give you your choice from two or more distinct functions.

name: modal inverse choices
description: Spells where the modal options are: X, Mirror of X

name: morbid
description: Effects that care about at least one creature dying this turn.

name: more expensive than mv
description: The actual cost you pay for an effect (or for an alternate mode) will often be higher than the card's mana value.

name: morphling
description: Cards with flowstone and capacity to gain additional abilities or evasion.

name: moxen
description: Artifacts that cost {0} and tap for mana that have the "Mox" name. Named after the original Mox cycle from Alpha.

name: mulch
description: An effect that filters cards at the top of the library, putting remainders into the graveyard.

name: multicast
description: A spell that involves casting multiple spells. (Not copying, but actually casting.)

name: multi character card
description: This card represents multiple distinct identifiable characters on one face. Avoid using this for nameless mobs.

name: multiclass party member
description: Creatures that are each of the four party classes.

name: multiplayer
description: Cards that interact with all of the players in the game. They might scale with the number of players or affect the turn order for example.

name: multiple bodies
description: One-shot effects that create two or more creatures on resolution, with at least one being a token. For cards that repeatedly create tokens, see [repeatable token generator](repeatable-token-generator).

name: multiple targets
description: These cards can target more than one object or player. Not mutually exclusive with [single target instant/sorcery](/tags/card/single-target-instant-sorcery).

name: multi removal
description: Removal for more than one, but less than all.  See also [spot removal](spot-removal) and [sweeper](sweeper).

name: mutiny
description: Cards that can make multiple creatures under the same players control fight (or [bite](one-sided-fight))

name: mutual sacrifice
description: Everyone sacrifices something.

name: named choice
description: Players choose something, but the choices are given names, rather than choosing the effect itself.

name: named token
description: Tokens that have a specific name, rather than inheriting their name from their type line.

name: name matters
description: Effects that care about card names: different names, same name, specific name, etc.

name: namesake spell
description: Spells (or, mostly spells) with names that identify them with a specific named character.

name: naya ferocious
description: Cards that care about you controlling creatures with power 5 or greater.

name: nemesis-mega-cycle
description: A currently incomplete cycle of creatures printed in commander precons that provide a pseudo-targeted static ability that impacts a single opponent.

name: neo-regenerate
description: An activated ability that says "this gains indestructible; tap it" is the new Regenerate.

name: nevermore
description: Cards with a certain name can't be cast. See also [lobotomy](/tags/card/lobotomy), where certain cards can't be exist.

name: nightveil theft
description: Exile your opponent's stuff and cast it (and very similar variations on the concept).

name: nimble
description: "Can't be blocked by creatures with power 3 or greater", an opposite of [daunt](daunt) first seen on Amrou Kithkin and first named in Mystery Booster 2. See also [gives nimble](gives-nimble).

name: ninjutsu
description: Collection tag used to apply the various tags that comprise the keyword Ninjutsu. Should be 1-1 with kw:ninjutsu.

name: no-creature-type
description: Creatures without any creature types

name: no mercy
description: Cards that destroy creatures that damage you.

name: noncreature french vanilla
description: These noncreature, nonvehicle, nonspacecraft cards have only keyword abilities. (For vehicles and spacecraft, see [french vanilla](french-vanilla).)

name: noncreature typal
description: Cards that care about creature types, but not necessarily on a creature card—so they're compatible with the Kindred cards from Lorwyn, for example.

name: nonfunctional reminder counter
description: Effects that last forever tracked with a nonfunctional reminder counter, such that removing the counter makes no functional change to the tracked effect. See also [functional reminder counter](/tags/card/functional-reminder-counter).

name: non-integer
description: Card contains a non-integer value (any number that is not a whole number), whether it be in mana cost, rules text, P/T or elsewhere.

name: non mana ability mana
description: Abilities that produce mana, but aren't mana abilities (as defined by CR 605.1)

name: nonstandard-bestow
description: Bestow creatures that, when attached to a creature, do something other than exactly providing a P/T boost equal to their P/T and their other abilities.

name: noted tracked information
description: Cards that have you note something down in order to track it.

name: notorious templating
description: Cards with effects that have to be templated in such a way that they are confusing to read at first glance. They don't have to be complex effects in practice (such as Animate Dead), but require a large amount of text to function in Magic.

name: offcolor ability
description: Abilities with a mana cost outside the card's colors.

name: offcolor additional cost
description: Cards with an additional cost of mana beyond the card's own colors.

name: offcolor mana generation
description: Non-colorless cards that generate colored mana, but don't generate their own color of mana.

name: old banish templating
description: Banish effects in which the "exile" and "return" effects are separate linked abilities.

name: old blocking deathtouch
description: Before the keyword was made evergreen in Magic 2010, deathtouch was sometimes a triggered ability based on blocking. See also [old damage deathtouch](old-damage-deathtouch).

name: old damage deathtouch
description: Before the keyword was made evergreen in Magic 2010, deathtouch was sometimes a triggered ability based on damage. See also [old blocking deathtouch](old-blocking-deathtouch).

name: old fight
description: Before Fight was keyworded in Innistrad, it was a one-then-the-other procedure spelled out in oracle text.

name: old lifelink
description: Before lifelink was keyworded in Magic 2010, it was a triggered ability.

name: old mana burn clause
description: "Mana Burn" was a removed rule that made you lose life upon losing unspent mana. Older cards that gave you mana sometimes had clauses to prevent this.

name: old typeline
