description: Cards that intentionally bring back the old typelines after they were changed for the sake of nostalgia and/or humor. Commonly used for the "Summon" typelines over creatures

name: old ward
description: "When this gets targeted by a spell/ability an opponent controls, counter it unless they do X". Would probably be replaced by the ward ability now.

name: one-off
description: These are cards that have only one known printing of them, as a one-off celebration card. This tag isn't for specific one-off printings, only mechanically unique cards.

name: one-sided fight
description: A creature deals damage equal to its power to another. Like Fight, but without the chance to fight back. See also [fling](/tags/card/fling) which involves sacrificing the creature.

name: onomatopoeia
description: Boom! Crash! Kapow!

name: opaline effect
description: Whenever your stuff becomes the target of a spell and/or ability, you draw a card.

name: opponent-discard matters
description: Cards that reward you for making opponents discard cards from their hand. See also [hate empty hand](hate-empty-hand) for cards that throw a party in the aftermath.

name: orochi ability
description: Freeze creatures with combat damage. This ability was used on Orochi in Kamigawa.

name: other games matter
description: Interaction with other games you can see, have played, or will play.

name: outnumber
description: Deal damage to a creature based on your creature count.

name: overrun
description: Effects that grant trample and +P/+T

name: pacifism
description: A form of removal that prevents a creature from attacking, and usually also from blocking and/or activating its abilities.

name: painland
description: Lands that deal damage to the user upon tapping them for mana

name: pair-commander
description: These cards can be one half of a two-card pair of commanders

name: paper-compatible
description: Online-only cards that would work if printed in paper, albeit annoying to track or execute at times

name: paradox
description: Cards that care about casting spells from anywhere other than your hand.

name: pariah
description: Cards that redirect damage to themselves (Or to the unfortunate equipped/enchanted creature)

name: passive ability
description: Planeswalkers with non-loyalty abilities.

name: peek
description: Cards that give you access to hidden information (Yours or other players)

name: peek-hand
description: Take a quick look at a players hand

name: peek-library
description: Look at some number of cards from the top of libraries

name: perpetual aura
description: Auras that come back to your hand as they "fall off".

name: per-player
description: A spell or ability that scales to become more powerful with the number of players; i. e, "For each player, do X"

name: persecution effect
description: Some Creatures get +N/+N, while other creatures get -N/-N

name: personal-text
description: Cards referring to themselves or other objects in ways that personify then beyond a singular objectified ‘it’. Note: This is NOT for cards that previously referred to players with his or her and were oracled to use their.

name: phase manipulation
description: Cards that interact with the phases of a turn, usually by having players skip or gain additonal ones.

name: phasing
description: Cards that involve phasing, whether the action or ability.

name: phoenix with set's mechanic
description: A phoenix with one of the set's iconic mechanics or mechanical themes.

name: phyrexian mana ability
description: Cards whose activated abilities cost phyrexian mana

name: phyrexian mana cost
description: Cards where the casting cost includes phyrexian mana

name: phyrexian token
description: These tokens had Phyrexian variants added in Modern Horizons 2 errata.

name: pierce
description: Cards whose excess damage is dealt to their target's controller instead.

name: pile
description: Effects that make you put things in a pile for selection/grouping purposes

name: pile card
description: A "put things here" card.

name: pillage effect
description: Cards that loot/rummage and create Treasure(s).

name: pillowfort
description: Cards that disincentivise or make it harder for opponents to attack you

name: pinger
description: Effects that deal just 1-2 damage repeatedly.

name: pitch spell
description: Cards that let you discard or exile another card from your hand to cast for less or no mana

name: planeswalker deck staples
description: Cards that are formulaic includes of planeswalker decks: [tutors](/tags/card/pwdeck-tutor) to help you find the deck's planeswalker, and [sidekicks](/tags/card/pwdeck-sidekick) that benefit from having that planeswalker out.

name: platinum angel effect
description: Cards that stop you specifically from losing while preventing your opponents from winning.

name: player spotlight
description: Player Spotlight cards are Magic: The Gathering cards that celebrate the winners of the World Championships, similar to [Invitational Cards](invitational-card) that were made in the years 1997–2007.

name: play from top
description: Cards that let you play or cast the top card of your library. (Separate from [impulsive draw](https://tagger.scryfall.com/tags/card/impulsive-draw) which exiles it first.)

name: playtest forecast
description: These playtest cards previewed mechanics seen within 2-3 years of release, meaning they were being worked on at the time!

name: plunder
description: Sacrifice something else to draw cards.

name: poison mechanics
description: Cards and effects that interact with poison counters by name.

name: poison opponents
description: Gives poison counters directly to opponents, preferebly to all at once, without damage.

name: poisonous
description: Makes, or is, a creature that poisons a player when it hits them.

name: polymorph
description: Effects that remove a permanent and replace it with another random one.

name: portmanteau
description: Cards named with a portmanteau—a word that's a combination of two words.

name: potentially black border
description: Cards not intended for constructed play that could have been printed in a vintage/commander legal product instead if they were printed today.

name: potentially free
description: Cards that can be played without spending any resources (Mana, life, tapping creatures, etc). Cards must be able to be not free, to be potentially free.

name: power boost to all
description: Cards that provide power to all creatures you control. May be limited to certain types or colors.

name: power matters-individual
description: Effects which care about the power of one creature (or multiple creatures individually, as opposed to as a total).

name: power matters-total
description: Effects which care about the total power of some set of creatures.

name: power nine
description: Nine rare cards from Alpha considered to be the most powerful nine cards in the entire game.

name: precognition
description: Cards that let you freely look at the top card of your library. You know, others don't.

name: precognition engine
description: Cards let you see the top card of your library and conditionally play it. See also [future sight engine](/tags/card/future-sight-engine) for an older variant in this vein.

name: preexisting dnd background
description: Cards named after Dungeons & Dragons backgrounds that already exist! 🎲

name: prevent etb
description: Cards that prevent permanents from entering the battlefield.

name: prevent mass blockers
description: Cards that sweepingly prevent blocks from happening.

name: pridemate
description: Effects that put +1/+1 counters on something when you gain life.

name: probe
description: Draw three cards, then discard two cards. Not to be confused with [compulsive research](compulsive-research) or [sift](sift).

name: processing
description: Removes a card of your opponent's from the exile zone for value or to deny them resources.

name: promotes to commander
description: Cards that make things that aren't commanders, commanders.

name: protects-creature
description: Effects that can protect a creature, e.g. with protection,  hexproof, indestructible, etc.

name: provoke lite
description: "That creature must block this one this combat if able." It's like Provoke, but without the untapping. Also similar to [lure](lure) and [lure-limited](lure-limited).

name: pseudo-dethrone
description: Cards that give benefits if their owner is attacking the opponent/player with the highest life total

name: pseudo-equipment
description: Before Equipment arrived in Mirrodin, WotC experimented with a number of effects that granted boosts that stuck but could be transferred.

name: pseudo-fog
description: Effects that can protect you from an entire combat phase, similar to the card Fog, but using less conventional means than damage prevention.

name: pseudo-hexproof
description: Cards that do something very much like hexproof, but not hexproof.

name: pseudo-legendary
description: Cards that aren't typed 'legendary', yet still have rules that prevent multiple copies of them being in play simultaneously.

name: pseudo-leveler
description: Creatures with abilities that add incremental layers of growth.

name: pseudo-shroud
description: Prevents targeting from certain things from any player, including it's controller.

name: punisher
description: Effects that give you a choice of two damnations.

name: punny name
description: Names that are puns or otherwise a play on words

name: pure draw
description: Spells and abilities that let you draw (a) card(s) without discarding other cards or sacrificing other permanents.

name: pwdeck-sidekick
description: Cards from Planeswalker Decks that care about having the corresponding planeswalker out.

name: pwdeck-tutor
description: Cards from Planeswalker Decks that tutor for the corresponding planeswalker.

name: quadratic
description: Cards with some kind of effect increases to the second power. This could mean something like a power increase, token creation, or otherwise. See [exponential](exponential) for a different, but similar kind of effect.

name: quick attach
description: Abilities that instantly attach equipment and/or auras.

name: quick enchant
description: Abilities that attach auras.

name: quick equip
description: Non-Equip abilities that attach equipment.

name: quietus effect
description: Get hit and lose a sizeable fraction of your life.

name: quote name
description: Card names that are explicitly based on an existing quote.

name: rack effect
description: Players get burned or lose life for having a small hand.

name: radiate
description: Effects that copy spells and abilities targeting a single creature.

name: raid
description: Cards that have an upside if you attacked this turn.

name: rainbow land
description: A land that can net one or more mana of any color.

name: ramp
description: Effects that increase available mana for current or later turns.

name: rampage
description: A creature gets +N/+N for each creature blocking it

name: ransom
description: Cards that remove permanents unless a cost is paid.

name: real life animal name
description: Card names that match real world english animal names. "Manta ray" is accepted, while "Hurr jackal" is not. Adjectives can disqualify a card unless it is a part of their name. "Giant ladybug" is a species name and therefore accepted, while "Venomous dragonfly" is not.

name: reanimate
description: Return permanent cards from the graveyard to the battlefield.

name: reanimate-cast
description: Cast a card from your graveyard.

name: reanimate copy
description: "Brings back" a copy of a permanent card in a graveyard instead of the original. Could be a token copy, could be turning another card into a copy of it.

name: reanimate-creature
description: Return creature cards currently in the graveyard to the battlefield. Not to be confused with [cheat death](/tags/card/cheat-death), which interacts right as the creature dies.

name: reanimate-self
description: These cards can return themselves to the battlefield if they're sitting in the graveyard. Not to be confused with [cheat death-self](cheat-death-self), where the card returns immediately after dying.

name: recasting commander matters
description: These cards care about you recasting your commander, or the number of times it's been cast.

name: recurring suspend
description: Cards that have suspend and, upon resolving, suspend themselves once more.

name: recursion
description: Get stuff back from your graveyard to use it again. See: reanimate (to battlefield), regrowth (to hand), and restock (to library).

name: recursion from exile
description: Brings itself or other cards you own back from exile, regardless of what effect might have put them there. For effects that do this to your opponent's cards, see [processing](https://tagger.scryfall.com/tags/card/processing).

name: recycle
description: Sacrifice a thing to get another thing back from the graveyard.

name: red effect
description: Effects which are iconically red.

name: reflexive trigger
description: An ability that triggers based on actions taken earlier during a spell or ability's resolution.

name: refund
description: Immediately get mana or untapped lands back.

name: regenerates other
description: Cards with effects that regenerate other things (and possibly including themselves.)

name: regenerates self
description: Cards with effects that regenerate themselves.

name: regrowth
description: Effects that return cards from your graveyard to your hand.

name: regrowth-any
description: Bring back a card of any card type from your graveyard to your hand.

name: relaxed commander restriction
description: Some Playtest card commanders have rules that allow you to run certain cards regardless of the commander's color identity.

name: relentless
description: Cards you can have more than the usual number of in your deck.

name: reminder card
description: Cards that remind you of your game state.

name: removal
description: Get things off the table.

name: remove counters
description: Remove counters from stuff.

name: remove counters-other
description: Remove counters from your opponents or their stuff.

name: remove counters-player
description: Remove counters from players.

name: remove counters-you
description: Remove counters from your permanents or yourself.

name: removes-mm-counters
description: Remove -1/-1 counters from your creatures.

name: removes-mm-counters-self
description: Removes -1/-1 counters, but only from itself, not from other creatures.

name: renew
description: Exile this card from your graveyard: Do a thing. Doesn't need to be the specific Renew keyword, just cards that have an ability to exile themselves in the graveyard.

name: renown
description: Collection tag used to apply the various tags that comprise the keyword Renown. Should be 1:1 with kw:renown.

name: repeatable creature tokens
description: Repeatable ways to create creature tokens

name: repeatable crime
description: Repeatable ways to commit crimes by targeting an opponent or something they control.

name: repeatable draw
description: Draw cards repeatedly, no matter if you go up on cards or not

name: repeatable noncreature tokens
description: Repeatable ways to create noncreature tokens

name: repeatable token generator
description: Repeatable ways to create tokens

name: repeated keyword
description: Repetition or stacking of a keyword.

name: rescue
description: Bounce something you control.

name: rescue-land
description: Bounce a land you control back to your hand.

name: restock
description: Effects that return cards from the graveyard to the library. (Named in Maro's color pie 2017 article.)

name: restock-all
description: Cards that put your entire graveyard into your library

name: restock-any
description: Return any cards to your library from the graveyard, not caring about their types.

name: restock-self
description: Cards that restock themselves.

name: restores old rule
description: Cards that have an effect that effectively brings back a rule that was changed or removed

name: restricted attacker
description: Cards that can only attack in specific circumstances.

name: restricted blocker
description: Cards that can block, but only in specific circumstances.

name: retaliate to damage
description: Effects that retaliate to damage inflicted upon you, by benefitting you or punishing the opponent.

name: reveal hand
description: Makes players reveal their hands more permanently than [peek](peek-hand)

name: revolt
description: Effects that care if a permanent (or creature, etc) you controlled left the battlefield this turn.

name: rhyming name
description: Cards whose names contain a rhyme, or come very close to it.

name: rhystic
description: Effects that opponents can buy off if they pay mana.

name: ritual
description: Spells that add mana.

name: roll planar die
description: Effects that instruct you to roll the planar die. NOT for responding to its rolls.

name: roman numeral
description: Cards with a roman numeral in the name.

name: rule of law
description: Cards that limit the number of spells a player can cast each turn.

name: rules nightmare
description: Cards that are known for having interactions that are difficult or nearly impossible to resolve within comprehensive rules

name: rummage
description: Discard a card, then draw a card. Mainly red. See also [loot](/tags/card/loot), the blue version, which draws then discards.

name: rummage to library
description: Rummage with the discarded cards tucked in your library.

name: rupture spire
description: Not only does this land enter tapped, it also requires you to tap another land when you play it.

name: saboteur
description: Effects that trigger when you deal damage to an opponent.

name: sacrifice cost land
description: Lands that require you to sacrifice another land before they come into play.

name: scales with multiple
description: Cards that scale as you play more copies of themselves.

name: scales with power
description: Effects which scale with the power of one or more creatures.

name: scry
description: See also [scry-like](scry-like) for effects that are like scry, but don't use the keyword.

name: second draw matters
description: Cards that care when the player casting or controlling them draws their second card for the turn.

name: second spell matters
description: Cards that care when the player casting or controlling them casts their second spell for the turn.

name: seek-artifact
description: Seek for artifact cards.

name: seek-card
description: Seek for a card of any type.

name: seek-creature
description: Seek a creature card.

name: seek-land
description: Seek for land cards.

name: seek-mv
description: Seek cards with a certain mana value.

name: seek-noncreature
description: Seek for noncreature cards.

name: seek-nonland
description: Seek for nonland cards.

name: seek-self
description: Cards that seek other copies of themselves.

name: seek-to-battlefield
description: Seek a card onto the battlefield.

name: self-discard matters
description: Cards that reward you for tossing your own hand into the graveyard. For cards that reward you for having a small hand, see [hellbent](hellbent) and [heckbent](heckbent).

name: self-replacement effect
description: A resolving spell or ability partially or completely replacing one of its own effects.

name: set life total
description: Your life total becomes N. See also [life divider](/tags/card/life-divider) and [life doubler](/tags/card/life-doubler).

name: shade pump
description: Effects that give an arbitrary amount of +1/+1 until end of turn. See also [firebreathing] in red and [armoring] in white.

name: shakedown
description: Give me something nice, or my big thing might hit you.

name: shapechange
description: Set creatures' power/toughness, not necessarily a nerf. See also [humble](/tags/card/humble).

name: shardland
description: Lands that produce mana from a "shard" (three allied colours).

name: shares name with a mechanic
description: Regardless of whether it relates to that mechanic.

name: shockland
description: Lands which enter tapped unless you pay two life.

name: shrink
description: Nerf a creature's power, temporarily or otherwise. Not to be confused with [humble](humble).

name: siege (modal)
description: Named ongoing modal effect. Chosen when entering and tracked by players.

name: sift
description: Draw three cards, then discard a card. Not to be confused with [compulsive research](compulsive-research) or [probe](probe).

name: silence
description: Prevent opponents from casting spells, and possibly also active abilities.

name: single english word name
description: Cards named with a single word in English. This may be a compound word.

name: single target instant/sorcery
description: Instant/sorcery spells that may target only one target. This is materially important for some effects that care about single targets. Not mutually exclusive with [multiple targets](/tags/card/multiple-targets).

name: skip turn
description: Someone skips a turn. See also [prevent extra turns](/tags/card/prevent-extra-turns).

name: skulk
description: Creatures with power greater than X's power can't block it.

name: sleeping enchantment
description: Enchantments that permanently "wake up" and become creatures.

name: slith ability
description: Whenever a creature deals combat damage to a player, it gets a +1/+1 counter.

name: sliver-stackable
description: Slivers having abilities where more than one instance of the ability does provide additional benefit.

name: sneak
description: Put a permanent from your hand onto the battlefield. (With or without a requirement to sacrifice it later.) Not related to the Sneak keyword first seen in TMT.

name: sneak-land
description: Cards that let you put lands on the battlefield from your hand without playing them.

name: sneaky-self-trigger
description: Cards that are worded in a way that makes it easy to miss that they trigger one of their own abilities.

name: snowfall
description: Cards that care about snow permanents entering the battlefield, not the weather condition.

name: sol land
description: Lands that mimic the mana ability of Sol Ring: add two colorless mana.

name: soothsaying
description: Look at the top N cards of a library and rearrange them.

name: soul warden ability
description: Creatures entering the battlefield causes you to gain N life.

name: special action
description: CR 116: Special actions are actions a player may take when they have priority that don’t use the stack. These are not to be confused with turn-based actions and state-based actions, which the game generates automatically.

name: specter ability
description: Damaging the opponent strips cards out of their hand.

name: spite damage
description: When you deal me damage, I deal you damage right back.

name: splits on death
description: Cards with abilities that create multiple creature tokens when a creature dies.

name: spot removal
description: Removal for one target specifically.  See also [sweeper](sweeper) and [multi removal](multi-removal).

name: stalking
description: The "can't be blocked by more than one creature" ability, [as named by the designers](http://markrosewater.tumblr.com/post/178266363158). See also [gives stalking](gives-stalking).

name: staple with set's mechanic
description: A common card design that is related to one or more of the main mechanics in a set

name: starting player matters
description: Cards that care about or interact with who is the starting player.

name: start of game
description: Cards that are particularly effective when you start the game with them.

name: state trigger
description: Cards that use a state trigger, which is a particular and rare kind of trigger.

name: static effect in graveyard
description: Cards with static effects that work from inside the graveyard.

name: stock turn
description: Skip a turn now in exchange for an extra turn later.

name: storage land
description: Lands that can store up mana over time for later use.

name: storm count matters
description: Storm count is the number of spells cast in a turn, named for [the Storm mechanic](//scryfall.com/search?q=keyword%3Astorm). This tag tracks cards that care about that quantity, but also the number just any one player has cast.

name: storm-like
description: Cards exploring design space in the vicinity of the Storm mechanic. Maybe one of these will be the progenitor of a future fixed version of the mechanic?

name: strive
description: Cards that require paying an additional cost per additional target.

name: substance
description: Substance is an obsolete static ability that has no effect at all. It existed briefly only as a technical detail to support implementing a few cards in Magic Online's Mirage release.

name: sunburst
description: Artifacts enter with a charge counter for each color of mana spent to cast them; creatures enter with +1/+1 counters.

name: sunder
description: Remove everything attached to a permanent while possibly leaving the permanent behind. (Some of these taggings consider the permanent might be indestructible.)

name: super-menace
description: Because you can get more menacing than menace.

name: super-pridemate
description: Put +1/+1 counters equal to the amount of life gained.

name: support
description: Put a +1/+1 counter on each of up to N target creatures.

name: surge
description: Get a benefit if you've cast another spell this turn.

name: surveil
description: Cards that let you look at the top # cards of your library and put some into the graveyard.

name: swap removal
description: Sorry I messed up your stuff. Here's a thing in exchange. (See also [humble](/tags/card/humble).)

name: sweeper
description: _o\ Destroy all the things! See also [spot removal](spot-removal) and [multi removal](multi-removal).

name: sweeper-graveyard
description: Remove all cards from one or more graveyards

name: sweeper-one-sided
description: Sweepers that are, or have high potential to be, one-sided.

name: sword of x and y
description: Originally a slow cycle of swords that give an protection from an enemy coloured pairing and additional bonuses based on those colors, now an enduring archetype of magic.

name: symmetrical
description: Cards that affect the whole battlefield/all players in symmetrical, equal manner.

name: synergy-1/1
description: Cards that synergize with creatures that are specifically 1/1.

name: synergy-attacker-opponent
description: Cards that synergize with any attacker, as long as they're attacking an opponent of the card's controller.

name: synergy-color-every
description: Cards that want all five colors to be present.

name: synergy-counterspell
description: Cards that make countering spells and abilities better.

name: synergy-energy
description: Cards that care about obtaining or having energy counters, but not by spending them.

name: synergy-exile-cast
description: Cards that care about casting spells from exile.

name: synergy-exiling
description: These effects care about cards being exiled (as in the action).

name: synergy-graveyard-cast
description: Cards that care about casting spells from graveyards.

name: synergy-library-cast
description: Cards that care about casting spells from the library.

name: synergy-low-power
description: Effects that care about you having creatures with power below some boundary value.

name: synergy-multicolor
description: Cards that care about you using multicolored cards.

name: synergy-solo-attack
description: Cards that care about attacking with only one creature

name: synergy-surveil
description: Beneficial effects for the Surveil keyword.

name: synergy-tapped
description: Effects that care about tapped permanents. For cards that care about something *becoming* tapped, see [uninspired](uninspired).

name: synergy-vanilla
description: Synergizes with creatures that have no abilities.

name: take the initiative
description: Cards that take the initiative, allowing you to enter Undercity and venture further into it on future turns.

name: tap fuel-creature
description: Tap a creature to pay for/activate an effect

name: tapland
description: Non-basic lands which come into play tapped

name: tap outlet
description: A permanent that, while in play, allows you to tap another permanent as part of an ability cost. Includes effects like "Tap an untapped creature you control: [insert effect]." and "Tokens you control have "{T}: [insert effect]."

name: tappable enchantment
description: There's a design guideline that enchantments should never tap themselves (which also means no {T} costs.) These ones break that rule.

name: tax
description: Extract resources from your opponents when they try to do stuff, either by making them spend extra resources or by generating them for yourself.

name: tax attack
description: Cards that prevent players from attacking unless they pay a cost, usually mana

name: team matters
description: Cards designed for Unknown events that care about what team you've chosen.

name: text change
description: Cards that directly change the text of a card.

name: the doctor
description: Cards that represent The Doctor, the eponymous lead of Doctor Who.

name: theft
description: Effects that let you steal cards and resources from your opponent.

name: thingfall
description: Abilities that trigger when something (other than itself) enters the battlefield

name: thoughtseize
description: Opponent reveals their hand, and you choose a card for them to discard.

name: threaten
description: Gain control of a permanent until end of turn. For continuous theft see [theft-creature](/tags/card/theft-creature).

name: threshold
description: Cards that care about you having seven or more cards in your graveyard.

name: timing restriction
description: Spells with unusual restrictions on when they can be cast.

name: titan immortality
description: These cards are shuffled into your library as they hit the graveyard.

name: titan trigger
description: An ability that triggers on both entering the battlefield and attacking, similar to the Titan cycle from M11

name: token errata
description: These cards used to make a different type of token, which has since been updated. For type lines, see [Type Errata](type-errata)

name: tokenlink
description: Dealing N damage creates N tokens (or similar).

name: token version of a card
description: These tokens are identical to a card except for missing CMC. See the parent tag above for more information.

name: token versions of cards
description: Some cards create tokens identical to existing cards (except for CMC). See: [cards creating these tokens](/tags/card/creates-token-of-a-card),  [printed tokens](/tags/card/token-version-of-a-card), and [the cards recreated in token form](/tags/card/has-identical-token).

name: token without a card
description: No cards make these tokens, probably due to errata.

name: toll
description: Generate resources from your opponent's actions without them being able to pay it off.

name: tome
description: Providing incremental card advantage over time (usually by tapping to draw cards, although there are variants that [tutor](tutor), [impulsive draw](impulsive-draw), etc.) has been a staple effect for noncreature artifacts since the printing of Jayemdae Tome in Alpha.

name: torment
description: A stock punisher effect where you force an opponent to choose between discarding, sacrificing, or losing life.

name: tormenting voice
description: A family of two-card rummage spells.

name: toughness matters
description: Other than in, you know, the usual way.

name: transcendental life/damage
description: Damage or life expressed in transcendental numbers. (Yes, we know this means it's not actually fractional.)

name: transferrable aura
description: Auras that have a built-in way to move them between permanents.

name: transform-improvement
description: Cards that do the same effect but better, or the same effects but with extra when they transform.

name: transform-mirror
description: Cards that mirror themselves mechanically when they transform

name: tribute
description: Collection tag used to apply the various tags that comprise the keyword Tribute. Should be 1:1 with kw:tribute.

name: tricycle-land
description: Lands that can tap for three colors of mana and have the ability to cycle.

name: trigger from exile
description: Abilities that trigger from inside exile.

name: trigger from graveyard
description: Abilities that trigger from inside the graveyard.

name: triland
description: Lands which tap for three colors. For triland cycles specifically see [cycle-triland](/tags/card/cycle-triland).

name: trumpet blast
description: Instants that give +N/+0 to all your (attacking) creatures until end of turn.

name: tuck
description: Effects that put a spell or permanent into its owner's library.

name: tuck-outlet
description: Cards that let you put cards from your hand into your library.

name: tunneling
description: Creatures with power 2 or less (sometimes 3) can't be blocked. An evasion pattern found mainly in red.

name: turns off defender
description: Turn your defender into an attacker with this one weird trick!

name: tutor
description: Cards that directly search for a card from your library, and sometimes other locations as well.

name: tutor-artifact
description: Cards that tutor artifact cards.

name: tutor-artifact-colored
description: Cards that tutor colored artifact cards.

name: tutor-artifact-creature
description: Cards that tutor artifact creature cards.

name: tutor-artifact-equipment
description: Cards that tutor Equipment cards.

name: tutor-artifact-legendary
description: Cards that tutor legendary artifact cards.

name: tutor-artifact-noncreature
description: Cards that tutor noncreature artifact cards.

name: tutor-artifact-vehicle
description: Cards that tutor Vehicle cards.

name: tutor-augment
description: Cards that tutor cards with augment.

name: tutor-aura-creature
description: Cards that tutor Aura cards with “enchant creature”.

name: tutor-aura-curse
description: Cards that tutor Curse cards.

name: tutor-black
description: Cards that tutor black cards.

name: tutor-blue
description: Cards that tutor blue cards.

name: tutor-card
description: Cards that tutor any cards.

name: tutor-cast
description: Cards that tutor something and cast it immediately.

name: tutor-color
description: Cards that tutor by color.

name: tutor-copy
description: Tutors for something with the same name as something else

name: tutor-creature
description: Cards that tutor creature cards.

name: tutor-creature-ally
description: Cards that tutor Ally creature cards.

name: tutor-creature-assembly-worker
description: Cards that tutor Assembly-Worker creature cards.

name: tutor-creature-aurochs
description: Cards that tutor Aurochs cards.

name: tutor-creature-colorless
description: Cards that tutor colorless creature cards.

name: tutor-creature-construct
description: Cards that tutor Construct cards.

name: tutor-creature-deathtouch
description: Cards that tutor creature cards with deathtouch.

name: tutor-creature-demon
description: Cards that tutor Demon creature cards.

name: tutor-creature-dinosaur
description: Cards that tutor Dinosaur cards.

name: tutor-creature-dragon
description: Cards that tutor Dragon creature cards.

name: tutor-creature-dwarf
description: Cards that tutor Dwarf cards.

name: tutor-creature-eldrazi
description: Cards that tutor Eldrazi cards.

name: tutor-creature-elemental
description: Cards that tutor Elemental cards.

name: tutor-creature-elf
description: Cards that tutor Elf creature cards.

name: tutor-creature-embalm
description: Cards that tutor creature cards with embalm.

name: tutor-creature-eternalize
description: Cards that tutor creature cards with eternalize.

name: tutor-creature-faerie
description: Cards that tutor Faerie cards.

name: tutor-creature-flying
description: Cards that tutor creature cards with flying.

name: tutor-creature-giant
description: Cards that tutor Giant cards.

name: tutor-creature-goblin
description: Cards that tutor Goblin cards.

name: tutor-creature-green
description: Cards that tutor green creature cards.

name: tutor-creature-hexproof
description: Cards that tutor creature cards with hexproof.

name: tutor-creature-kithkin
description: Cards that tutor Kithkin cards.

name: tutor-creature-legendary
description: Cards that tutor legendary creature cards.

name: tutor-creature-mercenary
description: Cards that tutor Mercenary cards.

name: tutor-creature-merfolk
description: Cards that tutor Merfolk cards.

name: tutor-creature-minotaur
description: Cards that tutor Minotaur creature cards.

name: tutor-creature-myr
description: Cards that tutor Myr creature cards.

name: tutor-creature-pirate
description: Cards that tutor Pirate cards.

name: tutor-creature-power
description: Cards that tutor creature cards with a certain power.

name: tutor-creature-rat
description: Cards that tutor Rat cards.

name: tutor-creature-reach
description: Cards that tutor creature cards with reach.

name: tutor-creature-rebel
description: Cards that tutor Rebel cards.

name: tutor-creature-sliver
description: Cards that tutor Sliver cards.

name: tutor-creature-spirit
description: Cards that tutor Spirit cards.

name: tutor-creature-toughness
description: Cards that tutor creature cards with a certain toughness.

name: tutor-creature-trample
description: Cards that tutor creature cards with trample.

name: tutor-creature-treefolk
description: Cards that tutor Treefolk cards.

name: tutor-creature-vampire
description: Cards that tutor Vampire cards.

name: tutor-creature-vanilla
description: Cards that tutor for creature cards with no abilities.

name: tutor-creature-wizard
description: Cards that tutor Wizard cards.

name: tutor-creature-zombie
description: Cards that tutor Zombie cards.

name: tutored by name
description: These cards are tutored by another specifically by their name. For the cards that tutor them, see [tutors by name](tutors-by-name).

name: tutor-enchantment
description: Cards that tutor enchantment cards.

name: tutor-enchantment-aura
description: Cards that tutor Aura cards.

name: tutor-flash
description: Cards that tutor cards with flash.

name: tutor-flashback
description: Cards that tutor cards with flashback.

name: tutor-from-opponent
description: Cards that tutor cards from an opponent's library.

name: tutor-green
description: Cards that tutor green cards.

name: tutor-host
description: Cards that tutor host cards.

name: tutor-instant
description: Cards that tutor instant cards.

name: tutor-instant-sorcery-arcane
description: Cards that tutor Arcane cards.

name: tutor-instant-sorcery-trap
description: Cards that tutor Trap cards.

name: tutor-land
description: Cards that tutor land cards.

name: tutor-land-any
description: Tutors with restricted effects that are able to search for any land.

name: tutor-land-basic
description: Cards that tutor basic land cards.

name: tutor-land-basic-forest
description: Cards that tutor basic Forest land cards.

name: tutor-land-basic-island
description: Cards that tutor basic Island land cards.

name: tutor-land-basic-mountain
description: Cards that tutor basic Mountain land cards.

name: tutor-land-basic-plains
description: Cards that tutor basic Plains land cards.

name: tutor-land-basic-swamp
description: Cards that tutor basic Swamp land cards.

name: tutor-land-desert
description: Cards that tutor Desert cards.

name: tutor-land-forest
description: Cards that tutor Forest cards.

name: tutor-land-gate
description: Cards that tutor Gate cards.

name: tutor-land-island
description: Cards that tutor Island cards.

name: tutor-land-mountain
description: Cards that tutor Mountain cards.

name: tutor-land-plains
description: Cards that tutor Plains cards.

name: tutor-land-snow
description: Cards that tutor snow land cards.

name: tutor-land-swamp
description: Cards that tutor Swamp cards.

name: tutor-legendary
description: Cards that tutor legendary cards.

name: tutor-monocolored
description: Cards that tutor monocolored cards.

name: tutor-multicolored
description: Cards that tutor multicolored cards.

name: tutor-mv
description: Cards that tutor cards with a certain mana value.

name: tutor-nonland
description: Cards that tutor nonland cards.

name: tutor-permanent
description: Cards that tutor for any permanent.

name: tutor-planeswalker
description: Cards that tutor planeswalker cards.

name: tutor-red
description: Cards that tutor red cards.

name: tutors by name
description: These cards tutor other cards by their name. For their tutor targets, see [tutored by name](tutored-by-name).

name: tutor-sorcery
description: Cards that tutor sorcery cards.

name: tutor-to
description: Tutor to specific zones.

name: tutor-to-battlefield
description: Cards that tutor something out of the library and straight onto the battlefield.

name: tutor-to-exile
description: Cards that tutor into exile for later use.

name: tutor-to-graveyard
description: Tutor cards into your graveyard.

name: tutor-to-hand
description: Cards that tutor cards to the player's hand.

name: tutor-to-top
description: Cards that tutor something and put it on the top of the library.

name: tutor-white
description: Cards that tutor white cards.

name: twiddle
description: Tap or untap something.

name: typal-choose
description: Choose a creature type with which to synergize.

name: typal coupling
description: Cards that care about two or more different creature types.

name: typal-lupine
description: Typal effects for Wolves and Werewolves.

name: typal-multi-sea-monster
description: Kraken, Leviathan, Octopus, Serpent. Sometimes merfolk join in too.

name: typal-neo-solo-attack
description: Kamigawa: Neon Dynasty coupled Samurai and Warriors as card types that care about attacking alone.

name: typal-share
description: Tribal effects that care about shared creature types.

name: type addition from none
description: Some creatures (Commonly artifact creatures) prior to the Grand Creature Type update were printed without Creature Types.

name: type addition human
description:
Creatures that weren't previously a human and just identified by their job, despite being a human, and then gained that type.

Creatures that had a strange type line from before standardisation, then jumped straight to being human aren't counted in this

name: type addition noble
description: Gained the Noble type, either after it was reintroduced with the big sweep at Eldraine launch, or at a later date.

name: type addition phyrexian
description: Gained the Phyrexian type, either after it was introduced with the big sweep at Modern Horizons 2 launch, or at a later date.

name: type addition sorcerer
description: These creatures gained the Sorcerer type with the release of Lorwyn Eclipsed

name: type errata
description: Cards that were printed with a type-line that has since been updated. For cards whose token generation has changed, see [Token Errata](token-errata).

name: type errata addition
description: Cards that received a new creature type because it fit them, or because they were lacking one before.

name: type errata cephalid
description: These were Cephalids, now Octopuses as of Modern Horizons 3.

name: type errata dinosaur
description: These creatures that were obviously dinosaurs the whole time, gained or changed to a Dinosaur typeline with the release of Ixalan.

name: type errata egg
description: These cards gained (Or re-gained in some cases) the egg type when it was reintroduced in Core set 2019

name: type errata genericise
description:
Sets of cards that were moved away from their creature type name to a more generic one, to allow them to work in typal decks better.

Cephalids, Naga and Viashino live in our hearts forever.

name: type errata hound
description: These were all hounds, now thanks to Ruff they're all dogs, as of Core set 2021

name: type errata lord
description: These cards used to have the now defunct "Lord" type

name: type errata mammoth
description: Mammoths are just elephants anyway.

name: type errata naga
description: These were Naga, now Snakes as of Modern Horizons 3.

name: type errata name self
description: These creatures used to just have Summon {CARDNAME} in their text box. Cf. [eponymous](eponymous).

name: type errata specific
description: Cards that had a very specific type line that was simplified to a larger group.

name: type errata summon creature
description: These creatures typelines at some point just read "Summon Creature", thanks to Portal!

name: type errata viashino
description: These were Viashino, now Lizards as of Modern Horizons 3.

name: type removal cat rakshasa
description: Rakshasa used to be Cat Demons or Cat Devils, but they have now lost the Cat type

name: un-color
description: Colors that don't exist in black border, like gold or pink.

name: undaunted
description: Undaunted spells cost 1 less for each opponent you have.

name: undergrowth
description: Cards that care about the number of creature cards in your graveyard.

name: undergrowth-all
description: Cards that care about the amount of creatures card in all graveyards.

name: un-design
description: Features characteristic of and unique to the world of un-sets.

name: un-forecast
description: Un-set cards that would later pave the way for black bordered mechanics

name: unheroic
description: Whenever you target something that's not yours, something good happens

name: uninspired
description: Effects that trigger when something becomes tapped. For cards that care about tapped permanents, see [synergy-tapped](synergy-tapped).

name: unique counter
description: These cards use a type of counter no other card does.

name: unique creature type
description: Creatures with one or more single creature types that are unique to them

name: unique cr reference
description: There is an entire rule in the CR just to cover this card and no other card.

name: unique keyword
description: This card uses a keyword action, ability, or variant thereof that's found on no other cards.

name: unique mana cost
description: This card's mana cost isn't found on any other card.

name: unique mana symbol
description: Cards with mana symbols that only appear on them.

name: unique p/t
description: Cards with a power/toughness not seen on any other card.

name: unique token
description: This token is created by only one card.

name: unique-type-exclusion
description: Cards that say "non-type" where no other card does that for that type.

name: unique type line
description: No other card has this same type line. Cards legal in one of vintage, legacy, or timeless keep the tag even if one or more cards not legal in those formats has that typeline.

name: universal type change
description: Cards that turn every type A into type B. Might be just one player or all, just the battlefield or not.

name: un-keyword
description: Keywords that are only seen in silver border.

name: unnoted tracked information
description: Spells or abilities that require tracking events without explicitly noting them. See also [noted tracked information](noted-tracked-information).

name: unprinted token
description: Cards whose tokens haven't yet been printed in paper.

name: un-set mechanics
description: Card tags showing mechanics that (at least nowadays) only show up in un-sets.

name: unstable killbot
description: Beep boop! 🤖 Unstable killbots share the same collector number but have different names.

name: unstable secret base
description: There are five Secret Base cards in Unstable. They share the same collector number but have different art, flavor text and watermark, matching each of the five Bablovian factions.

name: unstable variant
description: Cards in Unstable that have multiple versions with the same collector number.

name: unstoppable
description: Assign combat damage as though the creature weren't blocked. Also known as super-trample. See also [gives unstoppable](gives-unstoppable).

name: untracked indefinite effect
description: Effects that last forever but aren't tracked by anything. For these purposes we're not counting ETB clones.

name: un type line
description: A card with a type only found in silver border or on Acorn cards.

name: uril ability
description: An ability in which the P/T of one or more creatures can increase based on the number of aura attached to one creature outside of the aura's effects of being attached. See also [ethereal armor](ethereal-armor).

name: useless in singleton formats
description: Cards that have little to no use in formats that are Singleton (allowing only one of most cards save for basic lands) such as commander

name: vanilla equipment
description: Equipment that only changes power and toughness.

name: vanity card
description: Cards named after specific people or real things

name: virtual french vanilla
description: Creatures and vehicles that are french vanilla (having only keyword abilities) after entering the battlefield. They may have "enters the battlefield" or "on cast" trigger.

name: virtual legendary
description: This card represents a one-of-a-kind creature or object, and could conceivably have been legendary. Usually cards are in this category because they were printed before legendary existed or have a mechanical need to not be legendary.

name: virtual proliferate
description: Effects that sorta-proliferate some counters.

name: virtual vanilla
description: These creatures are effectively just vanilla after entering the battlefield. They may have an enters-the-battlefield or on-cast trigger.

name: wannabe dark confidant
description: Cards that riff off of Dark Confidant's iconic card advantage design.

name: warlord
description: This creature's P/T is equal to the number of creatures you control. Named in the Color Pie 2017 article.

name: weaker in singleton formats
description: Cards that have reduced use in formats that do not allow more than one card with the same name to be used, such as in Commander or Highlander.

name: wedgeland
description: Lands that produce mana from a "wedge" (2 allied colours and their shared enemy)

name: werewolf mechanic
description: Going a turn without casting spells transforms this to its back face. Casting two spells in a turn returns it to the front face. This also includes permanents that use the Daybound and Nightbound keywords to transform (see [day/night](day-night)).

name: wheel
description: Discard your hand, draw back up.

name: wheel-symmetrical
description: Wheel effects that effect each player, sometimes with no choice on whether they want to or not.

name: wheel-symmetrical-optional
description: Each player can choose whether to wheel or not

name: whirlpool
description: Wheeling to the library.

name: white elephant
description: Permanents that can be given to opponents and have detrimental effects.

name: wind drake with set's mechanic
description: A 2/2 flyer for 3 with one of the set's iconic mechanics.

name: wingman
description: Creatures with flying that give other creatures flying until end of turn when they attack.

name: wish
description: Effects which allow you to bring in cards from outside the game.

name: worship
description: If you would go below 1 life, go to 1 life instead.

name: you make the card
description: These cards came from community-driven "You Make the Card" events, where the Magic community works through multiple stages of voting to create a card.

name: young pyromancer ability
description: Whenever you cast an instant or sorcery spell, create a creature token.

name: y value
description: Cards that use "Y" for a value, usually  for "+X/+Y" effects on creatures.

name: zoo
description: Cards that create multiple tokens of different types.
